observations.recommendation: 1. We recommended that Management prioritize the implementation of the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts by strictly following the guidelines and procedures provided in COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020, to have reliable PPE balances that are verifiable as to existence, condition, and accountability.
observations.reason: The Municipality had yet to conduct a physical inventory count.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the ME, MPDC, and MBO properly coordinate in preparing the project plans and program of works of projects before the approval of the Annual Investment Plan and the Annual Budget.
We also recommended that the project plan and POW become the basis for setting definite and realistic timelines of programs, projects, and activities in the AIP.
We further recommended that the APP contain all the vital information about the projects to be implemented.
observations.reason: Management had yet to submit the relevant documents for substantial validation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management strictly follow the appropriate procurement procedures provided under R.A. No. 9184 and its revised IRR when hiring a technical consultant to ensure transparency, economy, and efficiency.
observations.reason: The transactions during the year involving consultants still need to be substantially validated.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management strictly comply with the accounting and reporting guidelines on the local roads asset management system and earnestly perform their respective duties and responsibilities set forth under COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated October 23, 2015.
We further recommended that, henceforth, the Inventory Committee conduct an annual physical count of all its Local Road Networks and the results thereof reported in the RPCLRN to be submitted to the Auditor and Accounting Unit not later than January 31 of each year. The RPCLRN shall be reconciled with the accounting records and the necessary adjusting entries be prepared before the statement date.
observations.reason: The Municipality had yet to finish the inventory count.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant exert extra effort to seek the necessary evidence or documentation to support the correction of the errors noted and, henceforth, improve monitoring and reporting systems, particularly in the preparation of the BRS, to ensure that reconciling items that need attention are addressed on a timely basis so as not to defeat the purpose of preparing the said report.
We further recommended that the Municipal Treasurer secure all stale checks and their supporting documents, cancel the same and furnish the Accounting Office with the original DVs for them to prepare a JEV restoring the Cash in Bank and crediting Accounts Payable and Prior Years’ Adjustment for stale checks less than two years and for those beyond two years, respectively, and henceforth, regularly monitor all outstanding checks so that written notices can be sent to the payees a month before these become stale.
observations.reason: Reconciling items amounting to ₱1,973,457.26, which existed before CY 2000, remained unadjusted due to unavailability of documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant conduct the required periodic reconciliation between the cashbooks and the subsidiary accounting records so that differences between the two records can be determined and corrected/adjusted right away to ensure the fairness of the presentation of the cash account in the financial statements.
observations.reason: The year-end balances still need to be evaluated to see if they were already reconciled.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Engineer ensure that sufficient and vital information, such as specific boundaries, stationing, landmarks, and symbols, are provided in the plans and drawings for road concreting projects.
We further recommended that, henceforth, Management unfailingly ensure strict compliance with the required detailed engineering activities in accordance with the provisions under Annex A of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184, giving utmost consideration to the deficiencies discussed above.
observations.reason: The documents for the year still need to be substantially examined to determine compliance.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management ensure that the LDRRM motor vehicles and heavy equipment be exclusively used for disaster/calamity response and rescue/relief operations in conformity with DILG MC No. 2021-004 dated January 18, 2021. Otherwise, refund/replenish the LDRRMF for the cost of the subject motor vehicles and heavy equipment, the appropriations for which shall be taken from the General Fund.
observations.reason: Trip tickets for the vehicles still need to be submitted to determine usage of the LDRRM vehicles.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction Management Council (MDRRMC), through the MDRRM Officer, review the MDRRMFIP and include all aspects such as, proper fund utilization and identification of programs, projects and activities (PPAs), time table, people, workload and others, in its preparation in order to accelerate the implementation of PPAs within the year so that the 70% Mitigation Fund of the LDRRMF will be fully utilized in accordance with its purpose.
observations.reason: Implementation for the year still needs to be evaluated at year-end.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant set up a SAGF to facilitate monitoring/tracking of the utilization of the Bayanihan grant pursuant to DBM LBC No. 125 dated April 7, 2020.
observations.reason: The BGCM had been fully utilized and liquidated so they can no longer use the monitoring tool.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management refrain from disbursing funds without appropriations in accordance with Section 305(a) of RA No. 7160.
observations.reason: Management had only submitted SB Resolution No. 23-042 which advised that the Local Finance Committee and the Mayor confer with the COA as to acceptable remedies to solve the matter.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management substantiate the propriety of the procurement of food items distributed to the households totaling ₱9,109,546.00 out of the BGCM, providing written explanations/justifications and documentary evidence to each of the issues discussed above, for further evaluation.
observations.reason: Management had not yet justified the subject issues.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant require the accountable officers handling the advances for petty cash fund, operating expenses, and payrolls to, henceforth, liquidate their cash advances strictly within the reglementary period set under Paragraph 5.1 of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997.
We further recommended that the Municipal Mayor, through the Municipal Accountant, exhaust all means to demand payment from the persons who had already retired from the government service or transferred to other government agency.
Lastly, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor file the request for authority to write-off dormant unliquidated cash advance of the late Mr. Nelson Bucol, to the COA Audit Team Leader (ATL) and/or Supervising Auditor (SA) in accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.
observations.reason: Demand letters had been sent to those who remained unliquidated. The request for authority to write off dormant receivable accounts was returned by the Auditor through a letter dated November 6, 2020, because Management failed to strictly comply with the conditions and requirements under paragraphs 7.4 and 8.3 of COA Circular No. 2016-005.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that, henceforth, Management furnish the Auditor with a copy of contracts together with the supporting documents within the reglementary period of five working days from perfection, in accordance with Section 3.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.
observations.reason: The submission of contracts was still beyond the reglementary period.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer require the issuance of ORs (AF No. 51) from the VMLP, Inc. and PCL, Inc. - Negros Oriental Federation for the payments made for membership dues/fees in CY 2019. We further recommended that the Municipality withhold future payments until the leagues comply with this requirement.
observations.reason: Payment to these Leagues in 2025 still needs to be verified.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that, henceforth, the Municipality properly identify the GAD issues and concerns and use the Fund to implement programs, projects and activities that are aligned with PCW, DILG, DBM, and NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 dated July 18, 2013.
observations.reason: The GAD database had yet to be completed hence, it could not be assured whether the GAD programs included in the Annual GPB were gender-responsive.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that in the preparation of its Annual Work and Financial Plan, the LCPC allocate more funds for PPAs that would promote children’s right and welfare and protect them against abuse, neglect, exploitation and violence as envisioned under RA No. 9344.
We further recommended that the Council prepare the Local Development and Investment Plans for Children, Local Code for Children, and Local State of Children’s Report as required under DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120 dated July 4, 2012.
observations.reason: The required reports under DILG MC No. 2012-120 had yet to be submitted.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the MAO (i) closely supervise the disposal program, seeing to it that complete records are kept including all details of births offspring, deaths and transfers; (ii) submit to the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant a monthly report of births of animals; (iii) facilitate the request for relief of accountability in case of death or disposal, or require payment of the value of the animal; and (iv) exert more effort in determining the current recipient and the status of the 22 heads of cattle.
We further recommended that the Municipal Treasurer post the monthly report of births of animals to the property records and the Municipal Accountant to the books of accounts.
We also recommended that the MAO, Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant reconcile their records taking into consideration the physical inventory as of December 31, 2017, and make necessary adjustments.
observations.reason: Inventory count and adjustments at year-end still needed to be substantially evaluated.

observations.recommendation:
a. The Inventory Committee was required to conduct the physical count of properties and reconcile the same with related property and accounting records and the municipal treasurer to maintain property cards in accordance with existing regulations and to locate the missing movable assets listed under Annex 1 of this report.
b. The Accountant to reclassify the fully depreciated and unserviceable assets from the Property Plant and Equipment to Other Asset accounts and to locate the disbursement vouchers of the unbooked motor vehicles, IT Equipment and furniture and fixtures so these can be recorded in the books.
observations.reason: Management had yet to finish reclassification of the needed properties.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: a. The Accountant and Treasurer exert all efforts to locate the missing disbursement vouchers amounting to ₱117,360.76 and the unidentified reconciling items/disbursements of ₱1,973,457.26 in order that these can be taken up in the books accordingly.
observations.reason: The unidentified reconciling items amounting to ₱1,973,457.26, which existed before CY 2000, remained unadjusted due to unavailability of documents.

observations.recommendation:
It was recommended that: a. The accountant should locate and trace/identify the remitting members from the supporting documents; reconcile and remit the same as soon as possible.
b. Management should give priority to this deficiency with the end in view of eliminating the same from the account balances to ensure reliable financial reports.
observations.reason: There still remained unremitted amounts pertaining to unidentified accounts. Waiting for the computation from GSIS of the unpaid premiums and loan deficiencies. The GSIS computation was still subject to further verification.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management should:
a. Require the municipal treasurer to immediately remit all taxes withheld for the accounts of the province and its barangays from various sources within the reglementary period on the 5th day after the end of the quarter.
b. Likewise, remit the CRMP shares after the end of each month pursuant to Article F of the Amended Municipal Ordinance No. 10-02 dated August 16, 2010.
c. Require the OIC-Municipal Accountant to look into the source documents of the ₱154,353.66 and prepare adjusting entries, if necessary.
observations.reason: Management had yet to release the CRMP shares due to the non-submission of supporting documents. The documents pertaining to the ₱154,353.66 had yet to be verified by the Municipal Accountant.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following:
a. Require concerned officials to refund immediately in full the disallowed payment amounting to ₱68,000.00. Be it a reminder that disallowance in audit is a joint and solidary liability of all officials involved in the processing and approval of such transactions;
b. Submit the lacking documentary requirements to prevent the suspension of ₱193,417.00 from turning into a disallowance; and,
c. Desist from attending training seminars organized and/or with the aid of private organizations, otherwise, this AOM shall serve as a stringent caution that we shall disallow the entire amount should there be any similar occurrences in the future. Take cognizance of DILG MC 2011-59 dated April 25, 2011 strongly discouraging conventions and the like from being held outside of the geographical island where the LGU is located.
observations.reason: Some officials had already started refunding the amount. Management had difficulty completing refunds for remaining officials.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management provide the auditor the revised and approved SARO specifying in detail the recipients of the PDAF so disbursed and the corresponding Memorandum of Agreement, if so warranted, for proper monitoring and evaluation of the fund transfers, otherwise, we shall be constrained to disallow said payments. Pending compliance, the subject transactions totaling ₱3,400,000.00 are hereby suspended in audit.
observations.reason: Management had yet to request a revised SARO from DBM.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the immediate refund in full of the amounts received in excess of what is authorized in DBM Budget Circular No. 2010-3 totalling ₱643,200.00.
a. Direct also the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Budget Officer to be vigilant and not to certify disbursements which go beyond the provisions of the law.
observations.reason: No request for write-off had been applied for the balance of ₱8,000.00 of one deceased employee (Ayawan) as Management will still try to collect the remaining balance.

observations.recommendation:
1.11 We recommended and Management agreed that:
1.11.1 The Municipal Accountant coordinate with the Municipal Engineer in
identifying the projects that have long been completed but are still
recorded in the CIP accounts;
1.11.2 The Municipal Accountant record the transfer of the cost of the
completed projects from CIP accounts to the respective PPE accounts
once these are identified;
1.11.3 The Municipal Accountant record the corresponding depreciation
expenses for the current and prior years;
1.11.4 The Municipal Accountant prepare individual subsidiary ledgers for each
project to ensure proper recording and monitoring; and
1.11.5 The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Engineer regularly
monitor the Municipality’s CIP accounts to ensure the timely transfer of
completed projects to the appropriate PPE accounts.

2.10 We recommended and Management agreed to identify all duplicate real
property entries in the assessment roll and require the service provider of the
iTax System to update and cleanse the database accordingly to ensure the
accuracy of the assessed values used for tax receivable recognition.
2.11 We further recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to prepare the
necessary adjusting entries to reflect the accurate balances of the RPT and SET
Receivable accounts in the financial statements once the amount of
overstatement of the RPT Receivable and SET Receivable is determined.
2.12 The Municipal Assessor stated that the Municipality had arranged for the iTax
System provider to update the system in March 2025. He further mentioned that since
his appointment in CY 2021, he has been actively addressing the issue of duplicated
entries within the system and continues to do so.

3.10 We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to:
3.10.1 Determine the total amount of penalties collected from RPT and SET that
were incorrectly credited to the respective receivable accounts instead of
being recognized as income, covering CY 2024 and prior periods;
3.10.2 Prepare the necessary adjusting entries to effect the corrections in the
financial statements to ensure accurate financial reporting; and
3.10.3 Henceforth, ensure that all penalties collected from RPT and SET are
properly recognized as income at the time of collection, in compliance
with Section 23 of the NGAS Manual and COA Circular No. 2015-009.

4.7 We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to:
4.7.1 Prepare the necessary accounting entries to rectify the recording of a P4.8
outdoor LED wall display costing ₱3,997,000.00, which was recognized as
Office Equipment (1-07-05-020) instead of Information and
Communication Technology Equipment (1-07-05-030), and to adjust the
corresponding depreciation expense accounts; and
4.7.2 Henceforth, refer to the description of accounts as a guide in recording
expenses in the books of accounts in adherence to COA Circular No.
2015-009 dated December 1, 2015, to avoid making erroneous accounting
entries in the books of accounts.
4.8 The Municipal Accountant submitted JEV No. 100-2025-04-024 dated April 25,
2025, reclassifying the outdoor LED wall display and correcting the depreciation
expense for the affected asset accounts.

5.11 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant reclassify the costs of various
supplies and equipment recorded in the accounts Other Supplies & Materials
Inventory, Other MOOE, and Other PPE to the proper accounts by making the
following adjusting entries:
Semi-Expendable Disaster Response and Rescue Equipment ₱109,595.00
Disaster Response and Rescue Equipment ₱198,489.00
Other Supplies & Materials Inventory ₱168,334.00
Other MOOE 40,250.00
Other PPE 99,500.00
5.12 We further recommended that the corresponding depreciation expense
computed for the account Other PPE be rectified, while the depreciation expense
for the account Disaster Response and Rescue Equipment be properly
recognized.
5.13 The Municipal Accountant submitted JEV No. 100-2025-04-023 dated April 25,
2025, reclassifying the payment of the above various LDRRM supplies and
equipment to their appropriate accounts, rectifying the depreciation expense
computed for the account Other PPE, and recognizing the depreciation expense for
the account Disaster Response and Rescue Equipment.

6.7 We recommended, and the Municipal Treasurer agreed to enforce the collection
of delinquent real property taxes by conducting intensive tax campaigns in every
barangay and by sending Notices of Delinquency to all delinquent taxpayers.
6.8 We also recommended that Management consider applying the remedies for the
collection of real property tax in accordance with the relevant provisions of RA
No. 7160.

7.13 We recommended and Management agreed that henceforth, the BAC
undertake procurements through competitive bidding in accordance with
Section 10, Rule IV of the IRR of RA 9184, except as provided in Rule XVI of
the IRR.
7.14 We further recommended and Management agreed to use Negotiated
Procurement–Small Value Procurement only if they can establish compliance
with the requirements specified in Annex “H,” Sections 48 and 53.9 of the 2016
IRR of RA 9184.
7.15 We furthermore recommended and the BAC agreed to strictly comply with the
posting requirements mandated by RA 9184 and its 2016 IRR.
7.16 The Municipal Budget Officer mentioned that it has been their practice as a 4th-class
Municipality to use SVP for the procurement of items not exceeding the
₱100,000.00 threshold, but they resolved to properly plan the Municipality’s
procurement activities to avoid similar errors in the future.

8.8 We recommended and the Municipal Mayor and the Municipal Engineer agreed
to inform the contractor of the deficiencies noted and require them to either
complete the remaining work items or refund the amount corresponding to the
uninstalled components, totaling ₱107,227.00.
8.9 We further recommended and Management agreed to enhance its monitoring
and validation procedures by ensuring that all work items are physically verified
and accounted for prior to certifying project completion and processing the final
payment.
8.10 Management sent a letter dated April 28, 2025, addressed to the contractor,
requesting that they resolve the identified deficiencies and complete the remaining
items in accordance with the specifications outlined in the POW.

9.8 We recommended and Management agreed to ensure strict compliance with the
provisions of Sections 116 and 121 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I,
regarding the requisition procedures for supplies and the reporting of their
issuance, respectively.

10.7 We recommended that Management submit the GPB and AR to the PPDO and
DILG Provincial Office within the prescribed period in accordance with Section
4.0 of PCW-DBM-DILG-NEDA JMC No. 2016-01 dated February 12, 2016.
10.8 We also recommended and Management agreed to strictly adhere to Item V of
COA Circular No. 2014-001 dated March 18, 2014, for the timely submission of
the said reports to the COA Audit Team.
10.9 We further recommended that Management ensure that the GAD AR is
accompanied by the required reports listed under Section C.8 (5) of the PCW-
DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01.

11.7 We recommended that the LSB ensure the proper documentation and timely
dissemination of the approved SEF Budget to the Municipal Budget Officer,
Municipal Accountant, and Municipal Treasurer, in accordance with the
requirements of Item 6.1 of DepEd, DBM, and DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s.
2017.
11.8 We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with
the LSB, ensure the prompt and regular submission of the quarterly and
annual SEF Utilization Reports to the DepEd Central Office, the Sangguniang
Bayan, and the DBM and DILG Regional Offices as mandated by the same
Circular.
11.9 Lastly, we recommended that the LSB establish a monitoring system to ensure
the timely submission of required reports to avoid future delays.

We recommended that Management take immediate action to comply with the requirements stated in DOH AO No. 2018-0001 dated January 26, 2018, to obtain the required license for the two units of ambulance. This will enable the Municipality to rectify the existing non-compliance and ensure that the ambulances operate in accordance with the necessary standards and regulations established by the DOH.
observations.reason: The Ambulance already has a DOH-License to Operate.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management submit the relevant supporting documents to the DVs covering the payment of financial assistance amounting to ₱9,913,900.00 related to the African Swine Fever outbreak, which should be based on the clear-cut policies and guidelines that they have yet to formulate and establish.
We further recommended that, henceforth, the Municipal Accountant ensure the completeness of the supporting documents for every transaction of the Municipality before payment is made in compliance with Section 4(6) of P.D. No. 1445.
observations.reason: Management is still in the process of formulating policies and guidelines regarding this. Certifications of Hog Death, and Swine Disease Investigation Form.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant submit the lacking supporting documents as required under Sections 9.1 to 9.1.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012, to enable the audit team to properly evaluate the accuracy and validity of the subject disbursements.
We further recommended that, henceforth, the Municipal Accountant ensure the completeness of the supporting documents for every transaction of the Municipality before payment is made in compliance with Section 4(6) of PD No. 1445.
observations.reason: Transactions for the year still need to be substantially examined to determine compliance.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management close their checking account with the PVB and appropriate the money for the implementation of programs and projects which are beneficial to the constituents of the Municipality, subject to the usual budgeting and accounting process.
We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant exert extra effort to locate the documents supporting the reconciling items, make representation with the bank for the furnishing of documents that could aid in the verification of the reconciling items, and prepare the necessary adjusting entries in the books of accounts.
observations.reason: Reconciling items still cannot be found.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management submit the required valid Permit to Purchase, Permit to Possess, and prior clearance or permit from the NTC prior to acquiring the 22 units of handheld radios and accessories in compliance with NTC Memorandum Circular Nos. 2-05-88 and 11-21-88. We further recommended that, henceforth, Management procure radiocommunication equipment from NTC-authorized and accredited dealers only and adhere strictly to the existing rules and regulations governing the acquisition of radiocommunication equipment.
observations.reason: Management still had not submitted the Municipality’s valid Permit to Purchase, Permit to Possess, and clearance or permission from NTC prior to the said acquisition.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management submit the following documents to support the payment of honoraria to the members of the MCT and MIAC:
a. An explanation of why the MIAC was created despite the absence of any stipulation in the MOA for the creation thereof;
b. Justification that the works rendered by the members of the MCT and MIAC were in addition to or over and above their regular workload; and
c. Computation of the honoraria paid to the payees. We further recommended that, henceforth, Management strictly comply all the conditions stipulated under the DBM Budget Circular No. 2007-2 dated October 1, 2007 before honoraria of personnel are granted due to assignment in special government projects.
observations.reason: Management had already discontinued giving honoraria to the MCT and MIAC members through salary deduction.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the personnel concerned or the end-user duly authorized by the Head of the Procuring Entity to completely and properly fill up the relevant and required information in the PO pursuant to Item B of COA Circular No. 96-010 dated August 15, 1996.
observations.reason: Transactions for the year still need to be substantially examined to determine compliance.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management strictly adhere with the rules on the conduct of procurement activities under RA No. 11494 and ensure that the documentary requirements are satisfactorily complied with pursuant to GPPB Resolution No. 18-2020 dated September 16, 2020 and GPPB Circular No. 01-2020 dated April 6, 2020.
We also recommended that Management submit an explanation on the deficiencies noted.
observations.reason: Management had been working on complying with the deficiencies.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management prepare a one-time report to the National Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council (NDRRMC), through the Office of the Civil Defense (OCD) on all donations received by the Municipality and furnish the Auditor a copy of the (a) Acknowledgement Receipts of the Donations In-Kind; (b) Proof of receipt by and distribution to the beneficiaries; and (c) Inventory of remaining undistributed items, if any, in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2020-009 dated April 21, 2020.
observations.reason: Management had yet to submit the report on donations to the OCD. Acknowledged Receipt of Donations for the Gokongwei Brothers Foundation as there was only a Delivery Receipt and Taxpayer’s Removal Declaration.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that in the distribution of relief goods, Management prepare the Relief Distribution Sheets which should contain the name and signature of recipients as well as the date of receipt, and such other necessary information, including the kind and quantity of items received to establish accountability.
observations.reason: Distribution was done during ECQ and there was no direct contact with the beneficiaries, thus there was difficulty in completing the RDS.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the Bids and Awards Committee to submit justification for the procurement of the items above the highest suggested retail price of the DOH. We further recommended that Management procure medical supplies based on the SRP set by appropriate government agencies to ensure that the Municipality get the most advantageous prices.
observations.reason: The BAC Chairperson commented that they were confronted with the prospect of shortage since the supply was very limited compared to the demand. In their haste and desperation to purchase medical supplies, they looked for other suppliers who could provide their immediate needs. They forgot to compare the prices with the prevailing market price and overlooked the possibility of overpricing Purchases for the year still need to be substantially validated.
The iTax system had been updated as of December 31, 2024. The Municipal Assessor and Accountant still had to finish the reconciliation of records between the two.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management take appropriate action to address the causes of delay in the implementation of projects under the 20% DF and to fast track the completion of the development projects in accordance with its targeted schedule.
observations.reason: Implementation of projects still needs to be evaluated at year-end.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management, through the Municipal Planning and Development Officer fast track the establishment of the GAD database storage of necessary information in aid of effective gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation in accordance with PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 to ensure that the PPAs are aligned with GAD-related undertakings.
observations.reason: The GAD database was still in the process of completion.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: a. Prioritize the allocation of funds for the establishment and operation of a sanitary landfill in accordance with Sections 40 and 41 of RA No. 9003. In the meantime, take action to convert the existing open dump site into a controlled dump and hasten its closure within a specific time frame; b. Enforce strictly the Municipality’s Ordinances relative to solid waste management and ensure 100 percent compliance thereof; and c. Involve all barangays to actively participate in the implementation of waste reduction and segregation at source in accordance with RA No. 9003.
observations.reason: The Sanitary Landfill had already been completed and operational. The Municipality had appropriated a budget.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the BAC post in the PhilGEPS and agency website, if any the Notice of Award, Notice to Proceed and Approved Contract as well as notify the losing bidders the result of the bidding pursuant to Sections 37.1.2, 37.1.6 and 37.4.2 of the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA No. 9184.
Conduct survey, site, construction materials, soils and foundation investigations on the road concreting projects, to ascertain the soundness and adequacy of materials/goods to be procured and invite only associations from a duly recognized group in a sector or discipline relevant to the procurement at hand.
observations.reason: Management had encountered problems in posting on PhilGEPS.

observations.recommendation: The Municipal Accountant and the Treasurer be required to return the unexpended balances of cash received for completed projects to the source national government agencies and/or remit the unexpended PDAF/CDF to the National Treasury pursuant to Section 6.7 of COA Circular 94-013 and the Supreme Court decision dated November 19, 2013 under General Ruling Numbers 208566, 208493 and 209251.
observations.reason: Balances of cash received from national government agencies totaling ₱396,552.41 and the national agencies totaling ₱396,552.41 had not been remitted/returned to the source agency because the national treasury required the identification of the source agencies which Management lacked.

observations.recommendation:
a. The Municipal Accountant was required to reclassify the recorded Advances to Officers and Employees totalling ₱61,876.68 to Other Receivables.
The proposed reclassification entries are as follows:
Other Receivables ₱61,876.68
Due from Officers & Employees ₱61,876.68
b. Proper coordination with the Department of Education (DepEd) be made to require the DepEd officials who are also members of the Local School Board to secure clearances from money and property accountabilities from the concerned local government agency, where they are assigned, to ensure that all outstanding cash advances and other property accountabilities are settled and/or returned first before they retire or transfer.
observations.reason: Management had already stopped granting cash advances to employees from other NGAs. Due to a lack of coordination between the LGU and DepEd personnel, the outstanding cash advances had not been fully settled. A reclassification had already been made on February 27, 2015 per Journal Entry Voucher Nos. 2015-001 and 2015-002.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:
a. The Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to maintain their respective property records for all real property.
b. The Mayor creates a committee to process the transfer of title of all acquired real properties to the municipal government.
c. The Mayor request the Sangguniang Bayan for appropriations to cover the cost of titling of all real properties.
observations.reason: Titling process is still ongoing. Still waiting for the titled real properties to be released.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended for the immediate recovery of the total amount of ₱64,107.40 from the various Fishermen Associations as indicated in Annex 8.
We further recommended that the LGU shall endeavor to outline programs and projects with long-term benefits which have a direct impact for the sustainable development of its coastal resources.
observations.reason: The PPAs did not include those with long-term benefits on the development of their coastal resources. The refund was made on installment basis through monthly payments by the Fishermen Association. The refund was made to the concerned Fishermen Association.

observations.recommendation: Required the municipal officials and employees to immediately refund in full the extra cash gift amounting to ₱1,332,000.00 for CY 2007. Henceforth, the local chief executive should ensure that the grant of similar benefits complies with prescribed guidelines especially insofar as observance of the PS limitation is concerned so that the disbursements will not be disallowed in audit.
observations.reason: There remained a disallowance balance of ₱18,000.00 related to a deceased employee which was under appeal with COA Regional Office No. VII.

observations.recommendation: Directed the Municipal Accountant to strictly comply with the reglementary period prescribed under Section 450 of the GAAM in the matter of the submission of monthly, quarterly and year-end trial balances.

1.10. We recommended that Management prioritize the implementation of the one-
time cleansing of PPE accounts by strictly following the guidelines and
procedures provided in COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020, to
have reliable PPE balances that are verifiable as to existence, condition, and
accountability.

2.19. We recommended that the ME, MPDC, and MBO properly coordinate in
preparing the project plans and program of works of projects before the
approval of the Annual Investment Plan and the Annual Budget.

2.20. We also recommended that the project plan and POW become the basis for
setting definite and realistic timelines of programs, projects, and activities in
the AIP.

2.21. We further recommended that the APP contain all the vital information about
the projects to be implemented.

3.8 We recommended that Management strictly observe the provisions of R.A. No.
7305 and its revised IRR for the grant of hazard pay to its PHWs.

3.9 We further recommended that Management secure the documentary
requirements for the payment of hazard pay to the PHWs as listed under COA
Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.

4.7. We recommended and Management agreed to immediately close their
checking account with the PVB and appropriate the money for the
implementation of programs and projects that are beneficial to the
constituents of the Municipality, subject to the usual budgeting and accounting
rules and regulations.

5.6. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor appoint or designate additional
DO to avoid granting multiple cash advances to the incumbent DOs. We also
recommended and Management agreed that no further cash advances be
granted to the DOs until their previous advances are settled or properly
accounted for.

6.16. We recommended and the Municipal Treasurer agreed that the AOs monitor
the effectivity and expiration dates of their respective fidelity bonds and see to
it that renewal thereof is made before the expiration.

6.17. We further recommended and Management agreed that henceforth, no AO
shall be allowed to perform their duties and responsibilities with expired
fidelity bonds and ensure that cash advances granted to AOs do not exceed the
maximum accountability covered by their fidelity bonds to protect the interest
of the Municipal government.

7.12. We recommended that Management strictly follow the appropriate
procurement procedures provided under R.A. No. 9184 and its revised IRR
when hiring a technical consultant to ensure transparency, economy, and
efficiency.

8.7. We recommended that Management take immediate action to comply with the
requirements stated in DOH AO No. 2018-0001 dated January 26, 2018, to
obtain the required license for the two units of ambulance. This will enable the
Municipality to rectify the existing non-compliance and ensure that the
ambulances operate in accordance with the necessary standards and
regulations established by the DOH.

9.13. We recommended that Management formulate and establish clear-cut policies
and guidelines for providing financial assistance to hog growers affected by the
African Swine Fever virus. These guidelines should encompass, but not be
limited to, clearly defined eligibility criteria for affected hog growers, amount
allocation for each classification of hog, specified documentation and reporting
requirements, a defined process flow for detection, reporting, diagnostic
requirements, culling, and compensation of affected hog growers, an
application process and approval mechanism, and monitoring, and evaluation
criteria.

10.9. We recommended that Management submit the relevant supporting
documents to the DVs covering the payment of financial assistance amounting
to ₱9,913,900.00 related to the African Swine Fever outbreak, which should be
based on the clear-cut policies and guidelines that they have yet to formulate
and establish.

10.10. We further recommended that, henceforth, the Municipal Accountant ensure
the completeness of the supporting documents for every transaction of the
Municipality before payment is made in compliance with Section 4(6) of P.D.
No. 1445.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant set up a SAGF to facilitate monitoring/tracking of the utilization of the Bayanihan grant pursuant to DBM LBC No. 125 dated April 7, 2020.
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented
observations.reason: Management commented that they did not receive a request for the creation of a SAGF, thus they continued to use General Journals and Journal Entry Vouchers as their monitoring tool for the utilization of the BGCM.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management refrain from disbursing funds without appropriations in accordance with Section 305(a) of RA No. 7160.
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented
observations.reason: Management said there was an immediate need for the service although it was not a reason for non-compliance. They further said that they should secure first Appropriation Ordinance to ensure that future disbursements of similar fund will be strictly in accordance with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management substantiate the propriety of the procurement of food items distributed to the households totaling ₱9,109,546.00 out of the BGCM, providing written explanations/justifications and documentary evidence to the Auditor for further evaluation.
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented
observations.reason: Management failed to justify the subject issues.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: a. The Municipality, through the MSWDO, apprise the DSWD, RO No. VII of the beneficiaries listed under the UCT program who are already deceased, and recommend for possible replacement from among the eligible family members of the deceased; and b. Management ensure systematic procedures while strictly adhering to existing guidelines set forth under DSWD Memorandum Circular No. 09 series of 2020 in the implementation of SAP.
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented
observations.reason: Management was unable to follow the existing guidelines from DSWD RO-VII because of time constraints. They also forwarded the recommendations to DSWD RO-VII for information.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the accountable officers or the end-user duly authorized by the Head of the Procuring Entity to completely and properly fill up the relevant and required information in the PO as required under Item B of COA Circular No. 96-010 dated August 15, 1996.
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented
observations.reason: Compliance is still ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management strictly adhere with the rules on the conduct of procurement activities under RA No. 11494 and ensure that the documentary requirements are satisfactorily complied with pursuant to GPPB Resolution No. 18-2020 dated September 16, 2020 and GPPB Circular No. 01-2020 dated April 6, 2020. We also recommended that Management submit an explanation on the deficiencies noted.
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented
observations.reason: Management focused more on addressing the emerging needs for the service and was not strict with the procurement process.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant reclassify the fund
transfer recorded to account Transfers for Project Equity Share/LGU
Counterpart (5-02-15-020) to the proper account Other Receivables (1-03-06-
990) by making the following adjusting entry:

Other Receivables ₱4,687,000.00
Prior Period Adjustment ₱4,687,000.00
(Account Prior Period Adjustment was recommended because the books of
accounts as of December 31, 2021 were already closed.)

We also recommended that Management require the DSWD KALAHI
CIDSS NCDDP-AF/BARANGAY POBLACION DISTRICT II, DAUIN for the
immediate liquidation of the subject fund transfer and prepare the necessary
accounting entry to take up the liquidation.

We further recommended that, henceforth, Management refer to the
description of accounts as a guide in recording expenses in the books of accounts
pursuant to COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015, to avoid
erroneous entries.

We recommended that Management close their checking account with
the PVB and appropriate the money for the implementation of programs and
projects which are beneficial to the constituents of the Municipality, subject to
the usual budgeting and accounting process.

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant exert extra effort
to locate the documents supporting the reconciling items, make representation
with the bank for the furnishing of documents that could aid in the verification
of the reconciling items, and prepare the necessary adjusting entries in the books
of accounts.

We recommended that, henceforth, Management comply with the
limitations on the use of the 20% Development Fund as outlined under the
DBM, DOF, and DILG JMC No. 1 dated November 4, 2020.

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant reclassify the cost
of water meters, fittings, polyethylene pipes, and other supplies and materials
recorded to account Water Supply Systems (1-07-03-040) to the proper account
Other Supplies and Materials for Distribution (1-04-02-990) by making the
following adjusting entry:
Other Supplies and Materials for Distribution ₱889,850.00
Water Supply Systems ₱889,850.00

We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant make the
necessary adjustment to Depreciation Expense and Accumulated Depreciation
accounts for the corresponding periodic allocation for depreciation recorded in
CY 2021 for the subject water meter, fittings, polyethylene pipes, and other
supplies and materials.

We recommended that Management stop the payment of additional
honoraria to the Barangay Health Workers (BHWs) pursuant to DBM Local
Budget Circular No. 63, Section 95 of R.A. No. 7160 and Article IX-B of the 1987
Philippine Constitution to avoid incurring illegal expenditures.

We recommended that Management refrain from hiring consultants
whose functions can be performed by regularly appointed officials of existing
offices of the LGU as set forth in laws, rules and regulations or in their position
description form to avoid the unnecessary duplication of works/functions.

We also recommended that, in the hiring of consultants, the pertinent
provisions of R.A. No. 9184 and its implementing guidelines are strictly complied
with. Moreover, Management should clearly define the consultants’ expected
output in their individual contract and require the latter to prepare their
monthly accomplishment reports vis-à-vis their respective definite expected
output to establish the necessity of the consultancy services they are employed
for.

We further recommended that, henceforth, payment of the services
rendered by consultants shall be properly supported with the documentary
requirements provided under Section 9.1.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated
June 14, 2012.

We recommended that Management submit the required valid Permit to
Purchase, Permit to Possess, and prior clearance or permit from the NTC prior
to acquiring the 22 units of handheld radios and accessories in compliance with
NTC Memorandum Circular Nos. 2-05-88 and 11-21-88.

We further recommended that, henceforth, Management procure
radiocommunication equipment from NTC-authorized and accredited dealers
only and adhere strictly to the existing rules and regulations governing the
acquisition of radiocommunication equipment.

We recommended that Management immediately stop charging to the
PCF the payment for the services rendered by the job order employee assigned
at the Municipal Rural Health Unit. Instead, charge the said expense to the
General Fund.

We further recommended that Management utilize the PCF strictly for the
expenses allowed under PhilHealth Circular No. 16, s. 2004 dated July 12,
2004.

We recommended that Management demand reimbursement from
Barangay Poblacion District II for the amount paid as fidelity bond premium of
Ms. Sindiong to be taken out from the DSWD KALAHI CIDSS program funds.

We further recommended that, henceforth, Management refrain from
paying the fidelity bond premium of accountable officers who are not officers or
employees of the Municipality to protect the interest of the government.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant reclassify the cost of one unit ambulance recorded to Disaster Response and Rescue Equipment to the proper account Other Transportation Equipment by making the following adjusting entry:
Other Transportation Equipment ₱3,094,000.00
Disaster Response and Rescue Equipment ₱3,094,000.00
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant had effected the reclassification through Journal Entry Voucher No. 2021-0010 dated May 12, 2021.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant require the accountable officers handling the advances for the petty cash fund, operating expenses, and payrolls to, henceforth, liquidate their cash advances strictly within the reglementary period set under Paragraph 5.1 of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997. We further recommended submission of Individual approved daily time records or daily report of attendance and copy of approved contracts of personnel under COS and JO scheme as applicable.
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented
observations.reason: Management had submitted some supporting documents; the Municipality sent its 6th Demand Letter to persons who had unliquidated cash advances but some advances remained unsettled. Lack of personnel also affected submission of all required reports.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the PHWs concerned to refund the COVID-19 Hazard Pay they received for their services rendered during the implementation of the ECQ. We also recommended strict adherence to DBM Budget Circular No. 2020-1 in granting the COVID-19 Hazard Pay.
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented
observations.reason: The PHWs concerned had refunded the COVID-19 Hazard Pay they received on top of the Hazard Pay granted under RA No. 7305 during the period of ECQ.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: a. Establish a procedure for identifying positions providing social services who shall be entitled to Hazard Pay with certification by Secretary of Health for PHWs and Local Chief Executive for social workers; b. Require all PHWs, Meat Inspector and SWDO to support their claims with proof of actual exposure to specific health hazards and occupational risks with certification from appropriate agencies; c. The Meat Inspector submit to the Auditor Certification from DOH attesting that his position is considered a PHW within the contemplation of RA No. 7305.
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented
observations.reason: Management acquired certifications (some lacked full required definitions) and provided some documentation; however, some certifications lacked the specific contemplation definition of RA No. 7305, and management did not provide proof of actual exposure for all concerned.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the recipient families/individuals of various donations-in-kind and relief goods should be based on the validated list of affected families. We also recommended that Management prepare Relief Distribution Sheets which should contain the name and signature of recipients as well as the date of receipt, and such other necessary information including the kind and quantity of items received to establish accountability.
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented
observations.reason: Management submitted some documentation but full validated lists and fully compliant Relief Distribution Sheets were not consistently provided.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management prepare a one-time report to the NDRRMC, through OCD on all donations received and furnish the Auditor a copy of the (a) Acknowledgement Receipts of the Donations In-Kind; (b) Proof of receipt by beneficiaries; and (c) Inventory of remaining undistributed items, if any, in accordance with COA Circular No. 2020-009 dated April 21, 2020.
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented
observations.reason: The Municipality submitted some reports but completeness and timeliness were not fully achieved.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that in the distribution of relief goods, Management prepare Relief Distribution Sheets which should contain the name and signature of recipients as well as the date of receipt, and such other necessary information, including the kind and quantity of items received to establish accountability.
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented
observations.reason: Distribution was during ECQ with limited direct contact, making completion of RDS difficult; Management provided partial documentation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management immediately stop using the LDRRMF for PPAs which are not properly chargeable thereto. We also recommended that Management appropriate/realign ₱3,289,500.00 in the 20% Development Fund and reimburse the LDRRMF for the cost of the ambulance and catering services; and adhere strictly to the provisions on the use of the LDRRMF.
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented
observations.reason: Management had formulated PPAs representing that the ₱2,119,765.12 were for expenses not among allowable uses of the LDRRMF. As of CY 2021, Management was still waiting for the surplus to be used for the refund.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management procure medical supplies based on the SRP set by appropriate government agencies to ensure that the Municipality get the most advantageous prices.
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented
observations.reason: Management had not fully implemented the recommendation; documents/references were required.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: a. The Local School Board (LSB) prepare their annual SEF budget based on the DepEd-approved SIP, and approve it through an LSB Resolution signed by the Chair and Co-Chair and majority of all its members; and b. The pertinent provisions on planning and budgeting for SEF under DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017 dated January 19, 2017 be strictly followed.
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented
observations.reason: Management had submitted documents but could not verify whether the SEF budget for CY 2021 was prepared based on the DepEd-approved SIP; submission and verification were incomplete.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant require the accountable officers handling the advances for the petty cash fund, operating expenses, and payrolls to, henceforth, liquidate their cash advances strictly within the reglementary period set under Paragraph 5.1 of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997. We further recommended that the Municipal Mayor file the request for authority to write-off dormant unliquidated cash advances of the late Mr. Nelson Bucol to the COA Audit Team Leader and/or Supervising Auditor in accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented
observations.reason: Management sent its 6th Demand Letter to persons who had unliquidated cash advances; some advances remained unsettled; request for authority to write-off dormant receivable was requested but not yet granted; lack of coordination and documentation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that, henceforth, they adhere strictly to the provisions under Sections 48 and 52 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume I, so that proper accounting treatment and control of the Petty Cash Fund may be established.
observations.managementAction: Returned unused petty cash fund amounting to ₱4,282.81 duly acknowledged by OR No. 5609552 dated 12/31/21.
observations.reason: Records showed that the Petty Cash Fund (1-01-01-020) account had a balance of ₱4,283.81 as of December 31, 2021, an indication that the procedures of the imprest system were still not fully followed though an OR returning the unused petty cash fund was provided.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that, henceforth, Management furnish the Auditor a copy of perfected contracts together with the supporting documents within the reglementary period of five working days from perfection, in accordance with Section 3.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant submitted copies of other perfected contracts for CY 2021 to the Auditor as required.
observations.reason: Some perfected contracts were submitted but not always within the reglementary period of five working days from perfection.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor appoint additional DO to avoid the granting of multiple cash advances to the incumbent DO.
observations.managementAction: Management started regulating cash advances to the designated Disbursing Officer.
observations.reason: The Municipal Treasurer's Office was understaffed making appointment/designation of additional DO difficult; records still showed DOs were allowed to draw multiple cash advances in one day.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management ensure that cash advances granted to the Disbursing Officer Designate do not exceed her maximum cash accountabilities covered by the fidelity bond; otherwise effect the increase of her fidelity bond commensurate to her accountabilities, in accordance with the Bureau of Treasury Circular No. 02-2009 dated August 6, 2009, in order to protect the interest of the Municipal government.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant ensured that cash advances were regulated to not exceed accounts covered by the DO's fidelity bond.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer require the issuance of ORs (AF No. 51) from the VMLP, Inc. and PCL, Inc.-Negros Oriental Federation for the payments made for membership dues/fees in CY 2019. We also recommended that the Municipality withhold future payments until the leagues comply with this requirement.
observations.managementAction: Management was waiting for issuance of AF No. 51 from VMLP, Inc. and PCL, Inc. – Negros Oriental Federation.
observations.reason: As of reporting, the leagues had not yet submitted the official accountable forms; Municipality withheld further payments pending compliance.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that, henceforth, the Local Chief Executive direct all department heads concerned to submit their respective duly accomplished AAPSI and assign a personnel to consolidate the same for submission to the Auditor within 60 days upon receipt of the AARs for the succeeding years.
observations.managementAction: Management submitted AAPSI of the audit recommendations contained in the AAR on March 29, 2022. They initially prepared the AAPSI but were unavailable to submit it to the Auditor’s Office earlier.
observations.reason: AAPSI was submitted 260 days instead of the prescribed 60 days from receipt of the AAR.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management submit the GAD Plan and Budget to the PPDO and DILG Provincial Office within the prescribed period in accordance with Section 4.0 of the PCW-DBM-DILG-NEDA JMC No. 2016-01 dated January 12, 2016.
observations.managementAction: Management submitted the GAD Plan and Budget to the PPDO and DILG Provincial Office on April 28, 2021 and June 17, 2021, respectively, after the prescribed period of March.
observations.reason: Submission was made after the prescribed period; hence, not fully compliant with timing requirements.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: a) The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer reconcile the difference between the RPT and SET Receivables accounts to avoid accumulation of unreconciled differences; b) The Municipal Assessor prepare and submit to the Municipal Treasurer the assessment roll containing a list of all persons whose real properties have been newly assessed or reassessed and the values of such properties on or before the thirty-first (31st) day of December each year; c) The Municipal Treasurer maintain Real Property Tax Account Register/Taxpayer’s index card and furnish the Municipal Accountant a duly certified list of taxpayers with the amount due and collectible for the current year as basis for recording the RPT Receivable and SET Receivable accounts; and d) The Municipal Accountant maintain subsidiary ledgers for accounts RPT Receivable and SET Receivable.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer had posted Real Property Tax Receipt Issued in CY 2019 in the Real Property Tax Account Register (RPTAR). Reconciliation work between MAO and MTO ongoing. Municipal Assessor had submitted assessment roll; Municipal Accountant awaiting final list.
observations.reason: Reconciliation ongoing; as of December 31, 2021, difference increased to ₱11,133,788.12; Municipal Accountant still waiting for the final list of taxpayers from Municipal Treasurer.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer enforce collection of delinquent real property taxes by conducting intensive tax campaign in every barangay and by sending Notices of Delinquencies to all delinquent taxpayers. We further recommended that the Municipality avail of the remedies in collecting delinquent real property taxes provided in Sections 256 to 260 of RA No. 7160 and that Notices of Delinquencies be sent and demand letters issued to delinquent taxpayers.
observations.managementAction: The MTO prepared Notices of Delinquency and sent demand letters to delinquent taxpayers; 16 taxpayers paid in full and some availed of compromise agreements; various follow-up actions taken including sending demand letters to delinquent taxpayers of specific barangays.
observations.reason: Some delinquent taxpayers paid or availed of compromise agreements; many delinquent accounts remain unpaid due to pandemic and financial hardship of taxpayers; ongoing collection efforts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management take appropriate action to address the causes of delay in the implementation of the projects under the 20% DF and to fast track the completion of the projects in accordance with its targeted schedule.
observations.managementAction: In CY 2021, 24 projects were completed; some projects were either for site validation or budget re-alignment to another project. The Municipality reported progress on several previously ongoing projects.
observations.reason: Some projects were completed while others remained ongoing or delayed due to requirements (e.g., awaiting ECC) or re-alignment.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management immediately stop using the LDRRMF for PPAs which are not properly chargeable thereto. We further recommended that the amount of ₱2,119,765.12 representing a portion of the LDRRMF spent for PPAs which were not properly chargeable thereto should be restored and refunded from the appropriations of the General Fund and 20% DF, respectively.
observations.managementAction: In CY 2021, Management had formulated PPAs representing that the ₱2,119,765.12 were for expenses not among allowable uses of the LDRRMF. Management indicated they were waiting for the surplus to be used for the refund.
observations.reason: Management had not yet restored/refunded the amount; waiting for surplus to be used for refund.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management properly identify the GAD issues and concerns and use the GAD fund to implement programs, projects and activities that are aligned with PCW, DILG, DBM, and NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 dated July 18, 2013.
observations.managementAction: Management represented that the preparation of GAD documents was ongoing; as of CY 2021, the Municipality had not completed the establishment of the GAD database of residents.
observations.reason: The Municipality had not yet completed the GAD database; establishing such database was resource-intensive and had not been fully accomplished.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management, through the Municipal Planning and Development Officer, fast track the establishment of the GAD database and storage of necessary information in aid of effective gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation in accordance with PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1.
observations.managementAction: In CY 2020, basic information of residents had been partially encoded; however, the establishment of a full GAD database remained pending due to resource constraints.
observations.reason: Full establishment of the GAD database was not completed due to cost and resource limitations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management request the Sangguniang Bayan to formulate and pass a GAD Code as soon as possible as a guide in the identification, implementation, monitoring and evaluation of GAD-related PPAs in accordance with PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-1 and PCW GAD Code Guidelines.
observations.managementAction: Management submitted a request to the Sangguniang Bayan to adopt a GAD Code; however, action by the Sangguniang Bayan was pending.
observations.reason: The Sangguniang Bayan did not take action on the request; no formal adoption was made.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that in the preparation of its Annual Work and Financial Plan, the LCPC allocate more funds for PPAs that would promote the children’s rights and welfare and protect them against abuse, neglect, exploitation and violence as envisioned under RA No. 9344. We also recommended that the Council prepare the Local Development and Investment Plans for Children, Local Code for Children, and Local State of Children’s Report as required.
observations.managementAction: Management submitted various documents indicating that services for children were provided as needed; however, there was no strong prioritization in expenditures for child protection services.
observations.reason: Only minimal projects for children were prioritized; many expenditures were AICS and honoraria; required reports under DILG MC No. 2012-120 were not fully prepared.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the MAO closely supervise the disposal program, ensure complete records are kept including details of births, offspring, deaths and transfers; that the Municipal Treasurer post monthly reports of births of animals to the property records and the Municipal Accountant; and that the MAO, Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant reconcile their records taking into consideration the physical inventory as of December 31, 2017 and make necessary adjustments.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Agriculturist submitted MAO and property records; some animals were accounted for upon assumption of office of the Municipal Agriculturist; reconciliation ongoing.
observations.reason: Only four out of 22 heads of cattle had been accounted for upon assumption of office; details and full reconciliation were incomplete; Municipal Treasurer did not submit full details.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management prioritize allocation of funds for establishment and operation of a sanitary landfill (SLF) and convert the existing open dump into a controlled dump and hasten closure within specific timeframe; enforce municipal ordinances on solid waste management; involve all barangays in active participation in waste reduction and segregation at source.
observations.managementAction: The Safe Closure & Rehabilitation Plan (SCRP) for the Municipality had been submitted to EMB-Cebu; DENR allowed temporary use of existing disposal facility; segregation enforced and non-biodegradable waste collected for disposal; Municipality awaiting DENR assessment for SLF construction; appropriations of ₱18M were made for initial SLF construction.
observations.reason: SLF construction pending DENR assessment and release of ECC; pandemic caused delays; some interim measures implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management immediately dispose of the ten units of unserviceable motor vehicles in accordance with procedures set forth under Section 79 of PD No. 1445 to avoid further deterioration and possible loss of value.
observations.managementAction: The unserviceable motor vehicles had already been disposed as per BAC Resolution No. 05-20-99B through a negotiated disposal to a lone buyer.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management write to PCSO requesting copies of the registration documents of the subject ambulance and make representation with LTO for immediate registration of the vehicle.
observations.managementAction: On December 14, 2020, Mayor Truita wrote to LTO-Dumaguete to seek assistance for the application of registration. Management initiated actions to secure necessary documents.
observations.reason: Some documents were not available; travel restrictions during pandemic made registration processing difficult; full registration pending.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management procure common-use supplies from PS-DBM and comply with Administrative Order No. 17 and DBM Circular Letter No. 2011-6 and submit the APP-CSE to DBM.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality inquired with PS-DBM for availability of supplies; however, PS-DBM had no delivery services and procurement from PS-DBM would entail additional costs and travel risks.
observations.reason: PS-DBM had limited stocks and no delivery services; pandemic impacted procurement options; Municipality procured locally when necessary.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the BAC post in PhilGEPS and on the municipal website the Notice of Award, Notice to Proceed and Approved Contract and notify losing bidders the result of the bidding, pursuant to the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.
observations.managementAction: The BAC posted notices in the municipal building in lieu of PhilGEPS due to poor internet connection; some training/assistance needs identified.
observations.reason: Technical difficulties and poor internet connectivity hindered posting on PhilGEPS; local posting was used as alternative while addressing connectivity/training issues.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Inventory Committee conduct physical count and reconcile with property records, locate missing items, reclassify fully depreciated and unserviceable assets, and update records.
observations.managementAction: Inventory of PPE had been partially updated; unserviceable vehicles disposed through negotiated sale; efforts to reconcile and update items ongoing.
observations.reason: Some PPE items could no longer be located; full physical count and reconciliation still ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended remittance of unexpended PDAF/CDF to the National Treasury and return unexpended funds to source agencies pursuant to Section 6.7 of COA Circular No. 94-013 and Supreme Court rulings.
observations.managementAction: Remittances were made: ₱957,407.92 deposited to the Implementing Agency on April 15, 2015 per OR Nos. 0088806-0088807. Other amounts required identification of source agencies.
observations.reason: Some balances were remitted in 2015; others lacked source identification and remained outstanding.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Accountant reclassify recorded Advances to Officers and Employees totaling ₱61,876.68 to Other Receivables and coordinate with DepEd to secure clearances from money and property accountabilities for DepEd officials who are also members of the Local School Board.
observations.managementAction: Reclassification entries were prepared and posted on February 27, 2015 (JEV Nos. 2015-001 and 2015-002). Demand letters and coordination efforts were initiated; some payments were received; others remained outstanding.
observations.reason: Some cash advances no longer collectible; coordination with DepEd required; partial recoveries made.

observations.recommendation: We recommended: a) Mayor instruct Municipal Treasurer and Accountant to maintain respective property records for all real properties; b) Mayor create committee to process transfer of title to municipal government; c) Mayor request Sanggunian for appropriations to cover cost of titling.
observations.managementAction: SB Resolution No. 20-049 dated May 12, 2020 was prepared to cover titling costs; gathering of documents underway; process ongoing.
observations.reason: Gathering of required documents for titling was ongoing; process not yet completed as of report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Accountant and Treasurer exert efforts to locate missing disbursement vouchers and unidentified reconciling items to adjust books accordingly.
observations.managementAction: Some reconciling items were traced and remitted; former Treasurer paid some amounts; reclassification entries were effected; unavailability of documents limited full reconciliation.
observations.reason: Unidentified reconciling items totaling ₱1,929,035.16 remained unadjusted due to missing documents predating 2000.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Accountant locate and remit the supporting documents and reconcile and remit the amounts ASAP, giving priority to eliminate the deficiency and ensure reliable financial reports.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant had already sent the service records of members to GSIS for reconciliation; GSIS provided partial computation of unpaid premiums and loan deficiencies which were subject to further verification.
observations.reason: Management was awaiting response from GSIS and further reconciliation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the Municipal Treasurer to remit all taxes withheld for the province and its barangays within the reglementary period on the 5th day after the end of the quarter; remit CRMP shares after the end of each month; require OIC-Municipal Accountant to look into the source documents and prepare adjusting entries if necessary.
observations.managementAction: Remittances of RPT and CRM shares amounting to ₱154,353.66 were done annually instead of within the 5th day after the end of the quarter; starting CY 2020 there were no CRMP shares due to pandemic.
observations.reason: Remittances were made but not in the prescribed timely manner; pandemic affected remittance schedule and CRMP collections.

observations.recommendation: We recommended immediate refund of the total amount of ₱185,764.50 from the recipients; those amounts spent for the various Fishermen Associations may be recovered from them.
observations.managementAction: The fishermen’s association had started paying the amount due through installment deduction from their monthly CRM share.
observations.reason: Refund made on installment basis from CRM shares; recovery ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended immediate recovery of the total amount of ₱64,107.40 from various Fishermen Associations and that the LGU outline programs and projects with long-term benefits for sustainable coastal resource development.
observations.managementAction: The association started payment of the amount due through monthly payments by the association; Municipal Accountant recorded recoveries in subsidiary ledgers.
observations.reason: Refunds being made via installment; full recovery ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended: a) require concerned officials to refund immediately in full the disallowed payment amounting to ₱68,000.00; b) submit lacking documentary requirements to prevent suspension of ₱193,417.00 from turning into a disallowance; and c) desist from attending training seminars organized by private organizations, otherwise future similar expenditures will be disallowed.
observations.managementAction: Some barangay officials made partial refunds; Management discouraged attendance at training seminars outside the island per DILG MC 2011-59; submission of missing documents was requested to avoid suspension.
observations.reason: Some refunds were made; other documentation remained lacking; trainings outside the island continued in some instances previously held.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management apprise the concerned agencies and request revised SARO specifying in detail the recipients of the PDAF so disbursed and the corresponding MOA, otherwise Auditor may be constrained to disallow said payments; pending compliance the subject disbursements were suspended in audit.
observations.managementAction: Management sent letters to the concerned Congressman and agencies requesting revised SARO and documentation; follow-ups were made in CY 2014 and 2015 but response was pending.
observations.reason: Pending response from Congressman/concerned offices; as of report the subject expenditures remained suspended in audit.

observations.recommendation: We recommended immediate refund in full of the amounts received in excess of the authorized limit totaling ₱643,200.00 and that Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer be vigilant to not certify disbursements beyond provisions of the law.
observations.managementAction: Some employees made partial refunds through payroll deduction; others (deceased or transferred) had arrangements; ongoing collection efforts.
observations.reason: Partial refunds made; some balances pending refund or arranged through payroll deductions or terminal leave settlements.

observations.recommendation: We recommended immediate refund in full of the extra cash gift amounts received in CY 2007 and ensure that similar benefits in future comply with prescribed guidelines.
observations.managementAction: Partial refunds effected through salary deductions; disallowance under COA process.
observations.reason: Some refunds made; matter under COA Regional Office review/appeal.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management ensure timely preparation and submission of CRAAF and monthly reports of accountability for accountable forms to the Auditor.
observations.managementAction: Reports of Accountable Forms were submitted monthly to COA per received files.
