observations.recommendation: We recommended that henceforth the Municipal Accountant comply with reglementary period prescribed under Section 450 of the GAAM for submission of monthly, quarterly and year-end trial balances.
observations.managementAction: Financial Statements were submitted; however, the Accountant failed to submit all year-end reports on the deadline due to lack of personnel.
observations.reason: Submission of required reports was affected by understaffing; some reports were eventually submitted though not within the reglementary period.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor appoint or designate additional DO to avoid the granting of multiple cash advances to the incumbent DO.
observations.managementAction: Appointing or designating additional DO was not possible because the Municipal Treasurer’s Office was understaffed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management ensure that cash advances granted to the DO Designate do not exceed her maximum cash accountabilities covered by her fidelity bond, otherwise, effect the increase of her fidelity bond commensurate to her accountabilities, in accordance with the Bureau of Treasury Circular No. 02-2009, in order to protect the interest of the Municipal government.
observations.reason: Verification of CY 2020 Report of Checks Issued showed that on April 1, 2020, DO-Designate Janine Padagdag was granted cash advances totaling ₱2,806,355.99 which exceeded her maximum cash accountability of ₱2,500,000.00.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that, henceforth, the Municipality withhold future payments until the leagues comply with this requirement.
observations.managementAction: The payments of membership dues/fees to the VMLP, Inc. and PCL, Inc.-Negros Oriental Federation were already made the payments when they received the audit observation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management submit the GAD Plan and Budget to the PPDO and DILG Provincial Office within the prescribed period in accordance with Section 4.0 of PCW-DBM-DILG-NEDA JMC No. 2016-01 dated February 12, 2016.
observations.managementAction: The Annual GPB FY 2020 was submitted to the PPDO only on October 22, 2019.
observations.reason: Management was not yet aware of the regulation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management immediately stop from using the LDRRMF for PPAs which are not properly chargeable thereto. We also recommended that, henceforth, the PPAs to be included in the annual LDRRMF Investment Plan should conform to the PPAs enumerated in NDRRMC, DBM and DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013 and DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-73 dated April 17, 2012. Further, the amount of ₱2,119,765.12 representing a portion of the LDRRMF appropriations spent for PPAs which were not properly chargeable thereto should be restored and refunded from the appropriations of the General Fund and 20% DF, respectively.
observations.managementAction: Management did not give any reason for failing to do so. In CY 2020, Management had not completed its AAR yet, hence, it could not be ascertained whether the LDRRMF spent portion of the LDRRMF was restored. Management had not completed its implementation in CY 2020.
observations.reason: (Reiterated under Finding No. 14, Part II of this Report.)

observations.recommendation: We recommended that, henceforth, the Municipality properly identify the GAD issues and concerns and use GAD Fund to implement programs, projects and activities that are aligned with PCW, DILG, DBM, and NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 dated July 18, 2013.
observations.managementAction: The amount of ₱2,119,765.12 representing that portion of the LDRRMF spent for ineligible PPAs in CY 2018 was not restored and refunded from the GF and 20% DF, respectively. The Municipality did not give any reason for failing to do so.

observations.recommendation: We recommended for the immediate refund of the total amount of ₱185,764.50. Consequently, those payments spent for various Fishermen Associations may be recovered from them.
observations.managementAction: Be it a reminder that disallowance in audit is a joint and solidary liability of the concerned officials involved in the processing and approval of such transactions. The Fishermen Associations had started paying through monthly deduction of the amount of ₱185,764.50.
observations.reason: The association started paying through monthly deduction from the fishermen’s CRM share.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant reclassify the cost of
one unit ambulance recorded to account Disaster Response and Rescue
Equipment to the proper account Other Transportation Equipment by making
the following adjusting entry:

Other Transportation Equipment ₱3,094,000.00
Disaster Response and Rescue Equipment ₱3,094,000.00

We further recommended that Management refer to the description of
accounts as guide in recording expenses in the books of accounts pursuant to
COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015 in order to avoid erroneous
entries.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant set up a SAGF to
facilitate monitoring/tracking of the utilization of the Bayanihan grant pursuant
to DBM LBC No. 125 dated April 7, 2020.

We recommended that Management refrain from disbursing funds
without appropriations in accordance with Section 305(a) of RA No. 7160.

We recommended that Management substantiate the propriety of the
procurement of food items distributed to the households totaling ₱9,109,546.00
out of the BGCM, providing written explanations/justifications and
documentary evidence to each of the issues discussed above, for further
evaluation.

We recommended and Management agreed that:
a. The Municipality, through the MSWDO, apprise the DSWD, RO No.
VII of the beneficiaries listed under the UCT program who are
already deceased, and recommend for possible replacement from
among the eligible family members of the deceased; and
b. They ensure systematic procedures while strictly adhering to existing
guidelines set forth under DSWD Memorandum Circular No. 09
series of 2020 in the implementation of SAP.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the personnel
concerned or the end-user duly authorized by the Head of the Procuring Entity
to completely and properly fill up the relevant and required information in the
PO pursuant to Item B of COA Circular No. 96-010 dated August 15, 1996.

We recommended that Management strictly adhere with the rules on the
conduct of procurement activities under RA No. 11494 and ensure that the
documentary requirements are satisfactorily complied with pursuant to GPPB
Resolution No. 18-2020 dated September 16, 2020 and GPPB Circular No. 01-
2020 dated April 6, 2020.

We also recommended that Management submit an explanation on the
deficiencies noted.

We recommended that the following documents be immediately
submitted to the Auditor to determine compliance with the conditions on the
grant of COVID-19 Hazard Pay set forth in Paragraph 4 of DBM Budget
Circular No. 2020-01 dated March 24, 2020, to wit:
a. Authority for the entitlement of the allowance;
b. Certificate of attendance during the ECQ period; and
c. IATF Resolution of the Risk Certification in the workplace
Likewise, we recommended that Management submit (a) Individual
approved daily time record or daily report of attendance of the 207 Municipal
personnel paid with COVID-19 Hazard Pay showing the official dates and hours
they physically reported for work at their respective offices or work stations
during the implementation of the ECQ period; and (b) Copy of the approved
contracts of personnel under COS and JO scheme.

We recommended that Management require the PHWs concerned to
refund the COVID-19 Hazard Pay they received for their services rendered
during the implementation of the ECQ. We also recommended that
Management adhere strictly to the provisions of DBM Budget Circular No.
2020-1 dated March 24, 2020 in granting of the COVID-19 Hazard Pay.

We recommended and Management agreed that:
a. They establish a procedure for identifying PHWs and plantilla
positions providing social services who shall be exposed to high risk or
low risk hazard including the duration of such exposure duly
approved by the Secretary of Health for PHWs and Local Chief
Executive for social workers, to support claims for hazard pay;
b. The Municipal Accountant require all PHWs, Meat Inspector and
SWDO to support their claims for hazard pay with proof of actual
exposure to specific health hazards and occupational risks with
certification from appropriate agencies and other documents required
under Section 5.8 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012;
and
c. The Meat Inspector submit to the Auditor Certification from DOH
attesting that his position is considered a PHW within the
contemplation of RA No. 7305.

We recommended that the recipient families/individuals of various
donations in-kind and relief goods should be based on the validated list of
affected families. We also recommended that in the distribution of relief goods,
Management prepare the Relief Distribution Sheets which should contain the
name and signature of recipients as well as the date of receipt, and such other
necessary information, including the kind and quantity of items received to
establish accountability.

We recommended and Management agreed to prepare a one-time report
to the National Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council (NDRRMC),
through the Office of the Civil Defense (OCD) on all donations received by the
Municipality and furnish the Auditor a copy of the (a) Acknowledgement
Receipts of the Donations In-Kind; (b) Proof of receipt by and distribution to the
beneficiaries; and (c) Inventory of remaining undistributed items, if any, in
accordance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2020-009 dated April 21,
2020.

We recommended that in the distribution of relief goods, Management
prepare the Relief Distribution Sheets which should contain the name and
signature of recipients as well as the date of receipt, and such other necessary
information, including the kind and quantity of items received to establish
accountability.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant require the accountable officers handling the advances for petty cash fund, operating expenses, and payrolls to, henceforth, liquidate their unliquidated cash advances strictly within the reglementary period set under Paragraph 5.1 of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997. We further recommended that the Municipal Mayor file the request for authority to write-off dormant unliquidated cash advance of the late Mr. Nelson Bucol, to the COA Audit Team Leader (ATL) and/or Supervising Auditor (SA) in accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.
observations.managementAction: The AAR sent its Demand Letter to the government service or agency. The request for authority to write off dormant other receivable accounts was returned by the Auditor through a Management letter dated November 6, 2020 because Management failed to strictly comply with the conditions and requirements under paragraphs 7.4 and 8.3 of COA Circular No. 2016-005.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that, henceforth, they would adhere strictly to the provisions under Sections 48 and 52 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume I, so that proper accounting treatment and control of the Petty Cash Fund may be established.
observations.reason: Records showed that the Petty Cash Fund (1-01-01-020) account had a balance of ₱28,907.31 as of December 31, 2020, an indication that the procedures of the imprest system were still not followed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that, henceforth, Management furnish the Auditor with a copy of contracts together with the supporting documents within the reglementary period of five working days from perfection, in accordance with Section 3.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.
observations.managementAction: Management sent its 6th Demand Letter to the AAR persons who immediately had transfer to other government agency. The Municipal AAR submitted copies of perfected contracts were initially started submitting to the Auditor within the reglementary period in CY 2019. However, not all copies of contracts in CY 2019 were submitted.
observations.reason: Some contracts were submitted to the Auditor within the reglementary period while not all copies of contracts were submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that, henceforth, the Local Chief Executive direct all their department heads concerned to submit their respective duly accomplished AAPSI and assign a personnel to consolidate the same for submission to the Auditor within 60 days upon receipt of the AARs for the succeeding years.
observations.managementAction: Management sent its AAPSI for the 2019 AAR on October 22, 2020. The AAPSI was prepared by the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant. The AAPSI was submitted 6 days instead of 60 days from the receipt of the AAR on August 7, 2020.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: a) The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer reconcile the difference between the RPT and SET Receivables account balances totaling ₱5,327,042.29 and henceforth, conduct periodic reconciliation, together with the Municipal Assessor, of the total collectibles from the RPT Receivables and SET Receivables accounts to avoid accumulation of unreconciled differences; b) The Municipal Assessor prepare and submit to the Municipal Treasurer an assessment roll containing a list of all persons whose real properties have been newly assessed or reassessed and the values of such properties on or before the thirty-first (31st) day of December each year; c) The Municipal Treasurer maintain Real Property Tax Register/Taxpayer’s index card and furnish the Municipal Accountant a duly certified list of taxpayers with the amount due and collectible for the current year as basis for recording the RPT Receivable and SET Receivable accounts in compliance with Section 20 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I; and d) The Municipal Accountant maintain subsidiary ledgers for accounts RPT Receivable and SET Receivable in accordance with Section 10 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume II.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant applied depreciation on the building since the RPT Assessor had increased to almost 300% because of the General Revision in 2016. The Municipal Accountant was still waiting for the final list of taxpayers from the Municipal Assessor. The Municipal Assessor already submitted the Real Property Receipt Account Issued in CY 2019 in the RPTAR.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer enforce collection of delinquent real property taxes by conducting intensive tax campaign in every barangay and by sending Notices of Delinquencies to all delinquent taxpayers. We further recommended that the Municipality avail of the remedies in collecting delinquent real property taxes provided in Sections 256 to 260 of RA No. 7160.
observations.managementAction: The MTO prepared Notice of Delinquencies for the remaining 52 taxpayers. 200 demand letters were sent to delinquent taxpayers of Barangay Maayongtubig with total tax due of ₱1,266,598.00. 16 taxpayers paid in full and 20 taxpayers availed of the compromise agreement.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management take appropriate action to address the causes of delay in the implementation of projects under the 20% DF and to fast track the completion of the development projects in accordance with its targeted schedule.
observations.managementAction: Out of the 31 current and continuing PPAs under the 20% DF, 13 were completed, three were on-going implementaion, 11 were implemented, while four were terminated.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that, henceforth, the Municipality maximize the use of the LDRRMF by implementing programs/projects/activities (PPAs) that will eliminate, lessen or limit adverse impacts of hazards and related disasters or calamities such as construction of embankments, rehabilitation/construction of evacuation centers, conduct of trainings on disaster preparedness and response, search, rescue and retrieval operations, and all other PPAs enumerated in Section 5 of the NDRRMC, DBM and DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013.
observations.managementAction: 77.80% of the total current and continuing appropriations was utilized in CY 2020 for the implementation of COVID-19 related projects.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management, through the Municipal Planning and Development Officer, fast track the establishment of the GAD database storage of the necessary information in accordance with PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 to ensure that the PPAs are aligned with GAD-related undertakings.
observations.managementAction: In CY 2018 the basic information was encoded for the establishment of GAD Database.
observations.reason: The establishment of GAD Database would cost a substantial amount and the Municipality could not afford it yet.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management request the Sangguniang Bayan to formulate and pass a GAD Code as soon as possible as a guide in the identification, implementation, monitoring and evaluation of GAD-related PPAs in accordance with PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-1 and PCW GAD Code Guidelines.
observations.managementAction: The Sangguniang Bayan did not take action on the request.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management undertake a concerted effort to implement PPAs outlined under the 20% DF and hasten the completion of the projects so that the constituents can enjoy the benefits that can be derived from the projects.
observations.managementAction: The four development projects with appropriations totaling ₱5,900,000.00 were already completed as of December 31, 2020.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that in the preparation of its Annual Work and Financial Plan, the LCPC allocate more funds for PPAs that would promote children’s right and welfare and protect them against abuse, neglect, exploitation and violence as envisioned under RA No. 9344. We further recommended that the Council prepare the Local Development and Investment Plans for Children, Local Code for Children, and Local State of Children’s Report as required under DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120 dated July 4, 2012.
observations.managementAction: Projects/programs for children were mainstreamed in the regular programs and services. Only four out of the 22 heads of animals and cattles had been accounted for upon assumption of office of the incumbent Municipal Agriculturist.
observations.reason: The Management did not prioritize child protection services; children were provided as need arose.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed that the MAO (i) closely supervise the disposal program, seeing to it that complete records are kept including all details of births, offspring, deaths and transfers; (ii) submit to the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant a monthly report of births of animals; (iii) facilitate the request for relief of accountability in case of death or disposal, or require payment of the value of the animal; and (iv) exert more effort in determining the current recipient and the status of the 22 heads of cattle. We further recommended that the MAO, Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant reconcile their records taking into consideration the physical inventory as of December 31, 2017, and make necessary adjustments.
observations.managementAction: The municipal accountant posted the monthly report of births of animals to the property records and the Municipal Accountant adjusted the books of accounts because the Municipal Agriculturist failed to submit the details/information. The WOABSLC was submitted by the MAO and received by the Municipal Accounting Office last January 25, 2019 for posting on the books of accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended: a) Prioritize the allocation of funds for the disposal establishment and operation of a sanitary landfill in accordance with both the Executive & Legislative procedures; In the meantime, take action to convert the existing open dump site into a controlled dump and hasten its closure within a specific time frame; b) Enforce strictly the Municipality’s Ordinances relative to solid waste management and ensure 100 percent compliance thereof; and c) Involve all barangays to actively participate in the implementation of waste reduction and segregation at source in accordance with RA No. 9003.
observations.managementAction: Segregation was strictly enforced and only non-biodegradable waste was collected for disposal. The final site assessment and facility had been approved by EMB-Cebu. The LGU is now waiting for the schedule of deliberation with the National Legislative Department and submitted the Residual Containment Area (RCA) to EMB which did not push thru due to the pandemic. The municipality appropriated ₱18M for initial construction of the sanitary landfill.
observations.reason: The facility had been approved by EMB-Cebu but the LGU is waiting for the schedule of deliberation with DENR which did not push through due to the pandemic.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LDRRM Officer submit the Monthly Report on the Sources and Utilization of Disaster Risk Reduction Management on or before the 15th day after the end of each month to the Office of the Auditor pursuant to Section 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 to monitor disbursements.
observations.managementAction: The LDRRM Officer prepared a supplemental LDRRMFIP for the prior years’ unexpended balances which were appropriated for the implementation of COVID-19 related activities. Monthly reports on the Sources and Utilization were submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management immediately dispose of the ten units of unserviceable motor vehicles in accordance with the procedures set forth under Section 79 of PD No. 1445 to avoid further deterioration and possible loss of value.
observations.managementAction: Inspection and appraisal and bidding were conducted by the DPWH Equipment Division. Two public biddings were conducted but both failed. All motor items were subjected to negotiated disposal.
observations.reason: Some PPE items could no longer be located. Unserviceable vehicles were available for disposal and were negotiated.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management write the PCSO and request for copies of the registration documents of the subject ambulance. We recommended further that they make representation with LTO, Dumaguete City, for the immediate registration of the vehicle.
observations.managementAction: On December 14, 2020, Mayor Truita wrote a letter to LTO-Dumaguete Office to seek assistance for the application for registration of the subject vehicle. Documents pertaining to the registration of the donated ambulance were submitted to the Office of the Mayor for processing of the registration.
observations.reason: Lack of personnel who could focus on the processing of the registration. The registration was submitted to the Office of the Mayor for processing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality procure common-use supplies from the PS-DBM and, henceforth, comply with the provisions of Administrative Order No. 17 dated July 28, 2011 and DBM Circular Letter No. 2011-6 dated August 25, 2011.
observations.managementAction: The availability of the Municipality with PS-DBM were inquired with PS-DBM for the availability of the supplies to be procured. The available stocks were limited. In addition, supplies purchased had to be picked up by the Municipality since PS-DBM had no delivery services, that would entail additional costs to the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the BAC post in the PhilGEPS agency website, if any, the bulletin board located at the entrance of the Municipal Building in lieu of posting in the PhilGEPS website. Also, conduct survey, site, construction materials, soils and foundation investigations on the road concreting projects, to ascertain the soundness and adequacy of materials/goods to be procured and invite only associations from a duly recognized group in a sector or discipline relevant to the procurement at hand.
observations.managementAction: The BAC posted in the agency website and the bulletin board located at the entrance of the Municipal Building in lieu of posting in the PhilGEPS website. Management encountered some technical problems during posting in the PhilGEPS website due to poor internet connection.
observations.reason: There were technical problems during posting in the PhilGEPS website due to poor internet connection.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: a) The Inventory Committee was required to conduct the physical count of properties and reconcile the same with related property and accounting records and the municipal treasurer to maintain property cards in accordance with existing regulations and to locate the missing movable assets listed under Annex 1 of this report. b) The Accountant to reclassify the fully depreciated and unserviceable assets from the Property Plant and Equipment to Other Asset accounts and to locate the disbursement vouchers of the unbooked motor vehicles, IT Equipment and furniture and fixtures so these can be recorded in the books.
observations.managementAction: Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE) was updated. Inspection and disposal were conducted. The municipal treasurer updated the property records. Some PPE items were already negotiated for disposal. Unserviceable vehicles were subjected to disposal but as of present these items were for negotiated disposal. The inventory of PPE was partially updated.
observations.reason: Some PPE items could no longer be located. The unserviceable vehicles were negotiated for disposal and the PPE was partially updated.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant locate the missing disbursement vouchers amounting to ₱117,360.76 and the unidentified reconciling items amounting to ₱1,973,457.26 in order that these can be taken up in the books accordingly. Also, coordinate with the source agencies to remit the unexpended PDAF/CDF to the Bureau of Treasury as required.
observations.managementAction: Due to lack of coordination between the LGU personnel, outstanding cash received from the national government agencies were not remitted to the source agencies because the national treasury required the identification of the source agencies which lacked.
observations.reason: The Municipal Treasurer initially remitted portions of the amount to the national agencies on April 15, 2015. The amounts were not remitted because the national agencies required the identification of the source agency which lacked.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: a) The Municipal Accountant was required to reclassify the recorded Advances to Officers and Employees totalling ₱61,876.68 to Other Receivables. b) Proper coordination with the Department of Education (DepEd) be made to require the DepEd officials who are also members of the Local School Board to secure clearances from money and property accountabilities from the concerned local government agency, where they are assigned, to ensure that all outstanding cash advances and other property accountabilities are settled and/or returned first before they retire or transfer.
observations.managementAction: The Journal Entry Voucher Nos. 2015-001 and 2015-002 were posted on February 27, 2015 for the reclassification. Due to lack of coordination between the LGU and DepEd personnel, outstanding cash advances amounting to ₱18,335.00 was not fully settled.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: a) The Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to maintain their respective property records for all real property. b) The Mayor create a SB committee to process the transfer of title of all acquired real properties to the municipal government. c) The Mayor request the Sangguniang Bayan for appropriations to cover the cost of titling of all real properties.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor wrote SB Resolution No. 20-049 dated May 12, 2020. The Municipal Treasurer prepared the deed of donation and other documents for processing. The Municipal Assessor submitted the registry of acquisitions. The municipality started the titling process and requested appropriations.
observations.reason: The documents were incomplete and there was lack of specific office/individual assigned to work on the transfer of title.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Accountant and Treasurer exert all efforts to locate the missing disbursement vouchers amounting to ₱117,360.76 and the unidentified reconciling items amounting to ₱1,973,457.26 in order that these can be taken up in the books accordingly.
observations.managementAction: The former Treasurer, Mr. Romer Deflino, paid the amount of ₱117,360.76. The unidentified reconciling items amounting to ₱1,973,457.26 remained unadjusted due to unavailability of documents.
observations.reason: The unidentified reconciling items remained unadjusted due to unavailability of documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: a) The accountant should locate and trace/identify the remitting members from the supporting documents; reconcile and remit the same as soon as possible. b) Management should give priority to this deficiency with the end in view of eliminating the same from the account balances to ensure reliable financial reports.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant had already sent the service record of members to the GSIS for reconciliation. The GSIS had partial computation of unpaid premiums and loan deficiencies, which were still subject for further verification.
observations.reason: To date, the GSIS had not acted on the request made by the LGU.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management should: a) Require the municipal treasurer to immediately remit all taxes withheld for the accounts of the province and its barangays from various sources within the reglementary period on the 5th day after the end of the quarter. b) Likewise, remit the shares after the end of each month to the barangays pursuant to Article F of the Amended Municipal Ordinance No. 10-02 dated August 16, 2010. c) Require the OIC-Municipal Accountant to look into the source documents of the remittances of the RPT and CRM to prepare adjusting entries, if necessary.
observations.managementAction: Remittances of the RPT and CRM to the barangays were done annually instead of the 5th day after the end of the quarter. Remittances of the RPT and CRM to the province and barangays were partially remitted. Documents pertaining to the remittances were yet to be verified.
observations.reason: Remittances were done annually instead of the prescribed reglementary period.

observations.recommendation: We recommended for the immediate recovery of the total amount of ₱64,107.40 from the various Fishermen Associations as indicated in Annex 8. We further recommended that the LGU shall endeavor to outline programs and projects with long-term benefits which led to sustainable development of its coastal resources.
observations.managementAction: The Fishermen Association started paying the amount due which were duly recorded by the concerned Accountant in the subsidiary ledgers prepared for the purpose.
observations.reason: The refund was made on installment basis through monthly payments by the Fishermen Association.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the following: a) Require concerned officials to refund immediately in full the disallowed payment amounting to ₱68,000.00. b) Submit the lacking documentary requirements to prevent the suspension of ₱193,417.00 from turning into a disallowance; and, c) Desist from attending training seminars organized by private organizations, otherwise, this AOM shall serve as a stringent caution that we shall disallow the entire amount should there be any similar occurrences in the future. Take cognizance of DILG MC 2011-59 dated April 25, 2011 strongly discouraging conventions and the like from being held outside of the geographical island where the LGU is located.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor had discouraged attendance to seminars organized by non-accredited private institutions. The concerned officials already made partial refunds.

observations.recommendation: We recommended for the immediate refund of the total amount of ₱3,400,000.00. Consequently, those payments spent for various Fishermen Associations may be recovered from them. Pending compliance, the subject transactions totaling ₱3,400,000.00 are suspended in audit.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor wrote the revised SARO to the former Congressman Henry Teves sometime in CY 2014 to follow-up. Another letter dated October 26, 2015 was sent to Congressman Teves as a follow-up.
observations.reason: To date, the LGU had not received the revised SARO. Follow-up letters were made.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the immediate refund in full of the amounts received in excess of what is authorized under DBM Budget Circular No. 2010-3 totalling ₱643,200.00. We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Budget Officer be vigilant and not to certify disbursements which go beyond the provisions of the law.
observations.managementAction: Employees concerned had started paying partial refunds through payroll deduction. One deceased employee (Ayawan) had a balance of ₱8,000.00 which could not be collected. Another employee (Rafal) promised to refund the money from his terminal leave pay.
observations.reason: No request for write-off had been applied for the balance of ₱8,000.00; partial refunds were made through payroll deduction.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the immediate refund in full of the extra cash gift amounting to ₱1,332,000.00 for CY 2007. Henceforth, the local chief executive should ensure that the grant of similar benefits complies with prescribed guidelines especially insofar as observance of the permissible limitation is concerned so that the disbursements will not be disallowed in audit.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant required the municipal officials and employees to immediately refund in full the extra cash gift amounting to ₱1,332,000.00 for CY 2007. The refund was applied through the 2007 salary deduction.
observations.reason: The disallowance was under appeal with COA Regional Office No. VII.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Treasurer and collectors prepare properly the Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms and submit the same after the end of each month as provided under Sections 50 and 51 of the NGAS, Volume II for easy reconciliation and verification of accountable forms and accountability thereof.
observations.managementAction: The Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms were prepared. The Consolidated Report was submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Accountant to strictly comply the reglementary period prescribed under Section 450 of the GAAM in the submission of monthly, quarterly and year-end trial balances.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant failed to submit the year-end report on the deadline due to lack of personnel.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant require the accountable officers handling the advances for petty cash fund, operating expenses, and payrolls to, henceforth, liquidate their cash advances strictly within the reglementary period set under Paragraph 5.1 of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997.

We further recommended that the Municipal Mayor, through the Municipal Accountant, exhaust all means to demand payment from the persons who had already retired from the government service or transferred to other government agency.

Lastly, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor file the request for authority to write-off dormant unliquidated cash advance of the late Mr. Nelson Bucol, to the COA Audit Team Leader (ATL) and/or Supervising Auditor (SA) in accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

We recommended and Management agreed that, henceforth, they would adhere strictly to the provisions under Sections 48 and 52 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume I, so that proper accounting treatment and control of the Petty Cash Fund may be established.

We recommended that, henceforth, Management furnish the Auditor with a copy of contracts together with the supporting documents within the reglementary period of five working days from perfection, in accordance with Section 3.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor appoint or designate additional DO to avoid the granting of multiple cash advances to the incumbent DO.

We recommended and Management agreed to ensure that cash advances granted to the DO Designate do not exceed her maximum cash accountabilities covered by her fidelity bond, otherwise, effect the increase of her fidelity bond commensurate to her accountabilities, in accordance with the Bureau of Treasury Circular No. 02-2009 dated August 6, 2009, in order to protect the interest of the Municipal government.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer require the issuance of ORs (AF No. 51) from the VMLP, Inc. and PCL, Inc. - Negros Oriental Federation for the payments made for membership dues/fees in CY 2019. We further recommended that the Municipality withhold future payments until the leagues comply with this requirement.

We recommended that, henceforth, the Local Chief Executive direct all department heads concerned to submit their respective duly accomplished AAPSI and assign a personnel to consolidate the same for submission to the Auditor within 60 days upon receipt of the AARs for the succeeding years.

We recommended that:

a) The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer reconcile the difference
between the RPT and SET Receivables account balances totaling ₱5,327,042.29
and, henceforth, conduct periodic reconciliation, together with the Municipal
Assessor, of the total collectibles from the Basic and Special Education Tax with
the balances of the RPT Receivables and SET Receivables accounts to avoid
accumulation of unreconciled differences;
b) The Municipal Assessor prepare and submit to the Municipal Treasurer an
assessment roll containing a list of all persons whose real properties have been
newly assessed or reassessed and the values of such properties on or before the
thirty-first (31st) day of December each year;
c) The Municipal Treasurer maintain Real Property Tax Account
Register/Taxpayer’s index card and furnish the Municipal Accountant a duly
certified list of taxpayers with the amount due and collectible for the current year
as basis for recording the RPT Receivable and SET Receivable accounts in
compliance with Section 20 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I; and
d) The Municipal Accountant maintain subsidiary ledgers for accounts RPT
Receivable and SET Receivable in accordance with Section 10 of the NGAS
Manual for LGUs, Volume II.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer enforce collection of
delinquent real property taxes by conducting intensive tax campaign in every
barangay and by sending Notices of Delinquencies to all delinquent taxpayers.
We further recommended that the Municipality avail of the remedies in
collecting delinquent real property taxes provided in Sections 256 to 260 of RA
No. 7160.

The Municipal Treasurer’s Office was in the process of preparing and
finalizing demand letters to be sent to all delinquent taxpayers of the Municipality.
Six taxpayers already entered into a compromise agreement with the Municipality.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to conduct a
thorough verification and analysis of the dormant receivable accounts and
provide for an allowance for impairment in accordance with the PPSAS or
PFRS and comply with all the conditions and requirements of COA Circular
No. 2016-005 for possible filing of request for authority to write-off dormant
receivable accounts by the Local Chief Executive to the Commission on Audit.

We recommended that Management take appropriate action to address
the causes of delay in the implementation of projects under the 20% DF and to
fast track the completion of the development projects in accordance with its
targeted schedule.

During the exit conference, the Municipal Engineer commented that
additional five projects were implemented.

We recommended and Management agreed to ensure that disbursements
out of the 20% DF conform to DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No.
2017-1 dated February 22, 2017 so that the desired socio-economic development
and environmental management outcomes as envisioned by the national
government may be achieved.

We recommended that Management undertake a concerted effort to implement the PPAs outlined under the 20% DF and hasten the completion of the projects so that the constituents can enjoy the benefits that can be derived from the projects.
observations.managementAction: AAR Solar Street 2017 Lighting amounting additional budget ₱200,000.00 for the Dauin Public Market is completed pending upon last June 2018. of Phase 1 of the project. The amount of ₱700,000.00 for the road opening in Barangays District III and Boloc-Boloc was put on hold because Management had not secured yet the deed of donation.
observations.reason: The Municipal Engineer noted additional budget amounting ₱200,000.00 for the Dauin Public Market is completed pending final completion of Phase 1 of the project. The amount of ₱700,000.00 for the road opening in Barangays District III and Boloc-Boloc was put on hold because Management had not secured yet the deed of donation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that in the preparation of its Annual Work and Financial Plan, the LCPC allocate more funds for PPAs that would promote children’s right and welfare and protect them against abuse, neglect, exploitation and violence as envisioned under RA No. 9344. We further recommended that the Council prepare the Local Development and Investment Plans for Children, Local Code for Children, and Local State of Children’s Report as required under DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120 dated July 4, 2012.
observations.managementAction: AAR Management agreed that the LCPC mainstreamed PPAs to regular projects/services to children.
observations.reason: Required reports were not prepared and submitted because Management failed to prioritize these.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed that the MAO (i) closely supervise the disposal program, seeing to it that complete records are kept including all details of births, offspring, deaths and transfers; (ii) submit to the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant a monthly report of births of animals; (iii) facilitate the request for relief of accountability in case of death or disposal, or require payment of the value of the animal; and (iv) exert more effort in determining the current recipient and the status of the 22 heads of cattle. We further recommended that the Municipal Treasurer post the monthly report of births of animals to the property records and the Municipal Accountant to the books of accounts. We also recommended that the MAO, Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant reconcile their records taking into consideration the physical inventory as of December 31, 2017, and make necessary adjustments.
observations.managementAction: AAR Management agreed that the MAO (i) closely supervise the disposal program, (ii) submit monthly reports, (iii) facilitate request for relief of accountability or require payment for dead/disposed animals, and (iv) exert more effort to determine current recipient and status of animals. The Municipal Treasurer to post monthly reports and Municipal Accountant to post to books; MAO, Treasurer and Accountant to reconcile records considering physical inventory.
observations.reason: The report on the reconciliation of the records was submitted on January 25, 2019 by the MAO and received by the Municipal Accountant but still not fully reconciled; the Municipal Animals and Breeding Stocks Ledger (WOABSLC) and Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant were still not reconciled as the required documents were not complete.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to ensure that only development projects are charged to the fund in accordance with DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017.
observations.managementAction: AAR Management agreed that the use of the multi-purpose hall for administrative purposes should be immediately stopped.
observations.reason: The municipal executive informed the Punong Barangay of Barangay Batuhon Dacu and the Punong Barangay agreed that the use of the multi-purpose hall for administrative purposes should be stopped.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to comply with the limitations and regulations on the grant of Uniform/Clothing Allowance, whether in cash or in kind, currently set at ₱5,000.00 per annum pursuant to the pertinent provisions of the GAA and Budget Circular No. 2012-1 dated February 23, 2012.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant complied with the limitations and regulations on the grant of Uniform/Clothing Allowance and will no longer distribute T-shirts and polo shirts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: a. Prioritize the allocation of funds for the establishment and operation of a sanitary landfill in accordance with Sections 40 and 41 of RA No. 9003 and in the meantime, take action to convert the existing open dump site into a controlled dump and hasten its closure within a specific time frame; b. Enforce strictly the Municipality’s Ordinances relative to solid waste management and ensure 100 percent compliance thereof; and c. Involve all barangays to actively participate in the implementation of waste reduction and segregation of at source in accordance with RA No. 9003.
observations.managementAction: AAR Management attended series of seminars on Waste Management and conducted Waste Analysis Characterization Study on October 18-21, 2018; drafted safe closure and rehabilitation plan for the disposal facility as required by DENR; complied with several activities with regard to waste segregation and currently operating a Residual Containment Area as temporary disposal site as advised by DENR; and allocated the amount of ₱7,900,000.00 for the procurement of equipment for the solid waste management program.
observations.reason: Some activities were conducted and plans drafted; the final safe closure and rehabilitation plan and full establishment of sanitary landfill are still in process.

observations.recommendation: Required that the Municipal Accountant ensure that GAD funds be used only for programs and activities promoting gender equality and women’s empowerment, and desist from charging non-GAD related projects to the GAD fund.
observations.managementAction: AAR Management agreed to stop the use of GAD funds for the payment of health insurance premium for indigent men and women, installation of street lighting facilities, and provision of water supply system and to henceforth include in their annual GAD Plan and Budget programs and activities which would promote gender equality only.
observations.reason: GAD Fund was still utilized for projects not related to GAD in CY 2018 because Management failed to adhere to the Auditor’s recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to: (i) refrain from distributing its prepositioned relief items for calamity purposes to clients under the different programs; (ii) monitor the withdrawal issuance of relief items; and (iii) henceforth, adhere strictly to the prepositioned provisions of RA No. 10121 and NDRRMC, DBM and DILG Joint Memorandum Circulars.
observations.managementAction: AAR Management complied and the Municipal Mayor instructed the MSWDO and the LDRRMO to refrain from distributing prepositioned relief items.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LDRRM Officer include in its LDRRMFIP the PPAs to be funded by the unexpended LDRRMF and to submit the Monthly Report on the Sources and Utilization of Disaster Risk Reduction Management on or before the 15th day after the end of each month to the Office of the Auditor pursuant to Section 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 to monitor disbursements.
observations.managementAction: AAR Management provided that the LGU submitted monthly reports.
observations.reason: The PPAs to be funded out of the unexpended balance were not included in the LDRRMFIP because Management failed to adhere to the Auditor’s recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality insure the project with the General Insurance Fund of the Government Service Insurance System (GSIS) in order that the government may be indemnified from any hazards incidental to acquisition, transportation and delivery of goods to the place of installation, and against risks of fire, earthquake flood, storm and other calamities.
observations.managementAction: AAR Management implemented the insurance and arranged for inclusion in the budget for the project and for inclusion in the municipal budget.
observations.reason: The municipality obtained the assessed amount for the project to be insured and for inclusion in the budget.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LGU conduct an industry forum and regular/special registration to gather data on the availability of jobs and jobseekers who are possible target beneficiaries and to expedite the preparation of the Workforce Development Plan (WFD).
observations.managementAction: AAR Management organized various skills training and conducted follow up on the approval of the Workforce Development Plan (WFD) from DILG Regional Office No. VII, Cebu City.
observations.reason: Various skills training were conducted to follow up 171 beneficiaries; the unexpended balance of ₱1,959,722.45 was refunded per Official Receipt No. 7656007 dated 11-4-18.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management immediately dispose of the ten units of unserviceable motor vehicles in accordance with the procedures set forth under Section 79 of PD No. 1445 to avoid further deterioration and possible loss of value.
observations.managementAction: AAR Inspection and appraisal of the vehicles were conducted by the DPWH Equipment Division.
observations.reason: The unserviceable motor vehicles were not yet disposed. Management did not give any reason for failing to do so.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant review the accounts payables which have been outstanding for more than two years, and if found to have no valid claims against the Municipality, cause the reversion of the same to the government equity.
observations.managementAction: Attended series of trainings and reverted the amounts: the former amounting to ₱1,973,457.26, which existed before CY 2000, was paid; the amount of ₱101,694.56 was reverted in December 2018 per JEV No. 2018-004, ₱16,867.40 was reverted on April 30, 2018 per JEV No. 2018-010 while the remaining amount of ₱56,459.15 was reverted on June 29, 2018 per JEV No. 2018-015.
observations.reason: Adjustment entries were made for portions of the amounts; remaining reconciling items of ₱1,973,457.26 could not be adjusted as documents were unavailable.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management write the PCSO and request for copies of the registration documents of the subject ambulance and make representation with and seek assistance from LTO, Dumaguete City, for the immediate registration of the vehicle.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality inquired with LTO Regional Office No. VII, Cebu City for the requirements needed for the processing of the registration and looked into the processing of registration of the donated ambulance.
observations.reason: Lack of personnel who could focus on the processing of the registration. The report/inspection was not signed by the Inventory Committee nor approved by the Local Chief Executive; thus registration was not yet completed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality procure supplies from PS-DBM and henceforth comply with the provisions of Administrative Order No. 17 of the DBM and related circulars.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality inquired with PS-DBM and procured supplies where PS-DBM had no delivery services; some supplies had to be picked up by the Municipality entailing additional costs to the procuring entity.
observations.reason: Availability of stocks with PS-DBM was limited and PS-DBM had no delivery services; thus procurement had to be arranged locally.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the BAC post in the PhilGEPS agency website, if any the BAC Notice of Award, Notice to Proceed and Approved Contract as well as notify the losing bidders the result of the bidding pursuant to Sections 37.1.2, 37.1.6 and 37.4.2 of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184. Conduct site survey, construction materials, soils and foundation investigations on the road concreting projects, to ascertain the soundness and adequacy of materials/goods to be procured and invite only associations from a duly recognized group in a sector or discipline relevant to the procurement at hand.
observations.managementAction: The BAC posted in the municipal bulletin board located in the entrance of the Municipal Building in lieu of posting in the PhilGEPS website. Conducted surveys on site, construction materials, soils and foundation investigations were carried out to ascertain the soundness and adequacy of materials/goods to be procured.
observations.reason: Posting in the PhilGEPS website was still in process due to slow internet connection.

observations.recommendation: a. The Inventory Committee was required to conduct the physical count of properties and reconcile the same with related property and accounting records and the municipal treasurer to maintain property cards in accordance with existing regulations and to locate the missing movable assets as listed under Annex 1 of this report. b. The Accountant to reclassify the fully depreciated and unserviceable assets from Property Plant and Equipment to Other Asset accounts and to locate the disbursement vouchers of the unbooked motor vehicles, IT Equipment and furniture and fixtures so these can be recorded in the books.
observations.managementAction: AAR The audit team received a copy of the RPCPPE on April 25, 2017 without complete information of the property, such as description, property no., unit value, remarks as to whereabouts of the property. In addition, the report was not signed by the Inventory Committee nor approved by the LCE.
observations.reason: Some properties could no longer be located; the report was incomplete and not approved by the LCE.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Treasurer return the unexpended balances of completed projects to the source national government agencies and/or remit the unexpended PDAF/CDF to the National Treasury pursuant to Section 6.7 of COA Circular 94-013 and the Supreme Court decision.
observations.managementAction: The Treasurer remitted the balances as follows: deposited to the Bureau of the National Treasury on April 15, 2015 per Official Receipt Nos. 0088806-0088807 for PDAF amounts. The municipal treasury remitted some amounts and identified the source agencies.
observations.reason: Some documents identifying the source agencies were lacking; hence some balances were not remitted earlier but were remitted subsequently when identified.

observations.recommendation: a. The Municipal Accountant was required to reclassify the recorded Advances to Officers and Employees totalling P61,876.68 to Other Receivables. b. Proper coordination with the Department of Education (DepEd) be made to require the DepEd officials who are also members of the Local School Board to secure clearances from money and property accountabilities from the concerned local government agency, where they are assigned, to ensure that all outstanding cash advances and other property accountabilities are settled and/or returned first before they retire or transfer.
observations.managementAction: AAR The proposed reclassification entries were prepared and recorded on February 27, 2015 per Journal Entry Voucher Nos. 2015-001 and 2015-002. The municipality coordinated with DepEd to secure clearances and some employee accounts were settled.
observations.reason: Outstanding cash advances amounting to ₱18,335.00 were not fully settled due to lack of coordination between the LGU and DepEd; some accounts are still outstanding.

observations.recommendation: a. The Mayor instructs the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to maintain their respective property records for all real property. b. The Mayor creates a committee to process the transfer of title of all acquired real properties to the municipal government. c. The Mayor requests the Sangguniang Bayan for appropriations to cover the cost of titling of all real properties.
observations.managementAction: The Mayor issued a memorandum creating a LGU committee for the titling of land owned by the municipality and the LGU had already initiated the titling process.
observations.reason: The committee was still processing the titling; some documents were being compiled.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: a. The Accountant and Treasurer exert all efforts to locate the missing disbursement vouchers amounting to ₱117,360.76 and the unidentified reconciling items/disbursements of ₱1,973,457.26 in order that these can be taken up in the books accordingly. b. For the other reconciling items, the Municipal Accountant take up the following adjusting journal entries: For the erroneous deposit: Debit Prior Year’s Adjustment 5,826.38; Credit Due to Officers & Employees 5,826.38. For the bank charges: Debit Prior Year‘s Adjustments 350.00; Credit Cash in Bank 350.00. For the Stale check: Debit Cash in Bank 33,313.85; Credit Accounts Payable/Other Appropriate Account 333,313.85.
observations.managementAction: The former municipal treasurer, Mr. Romer Deflino, paid the amount of ₱1,973,457.26 which existed before CY 2000. The accounts were adjusted in CY 2014 per Journal Entry Voucher Nos. dated July 8, 2014 and April 25, 2014. The other reconciling items were already taken up in the books in CY 2014 per JEVs.
observations.reason: Unavailability of documents prevented full reconciliation for some reconciling items; some adjustments were already made in CY 2014.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: a. The Municipal Treasurer send immediately demand letters to the concerned accountable agency/other agency officials/employees. b. If the employees concerned fail to settle their cash advances after due notice, instruct the municipal treasurer to withhold the payment of their salaries. c. Require in particular the Municipal Accountant and other concerned officials/employees to strictly observe the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 in the grant, utilization and liquidation of cash advances.
observations.managementAction: AAR Management sent demand letters to concerned accountable persons and some employees made partial refunds; the municipal treasurer withheld salary payments of some employees who failed to settle their accounts.
observations.reason: Some employees already refunded partially; others had not yet fully settled their accounts; some national government agency personnel were not required to secure clearance from the LGU, making collection difficult.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: a. The accountant should locate and trace/identify the remitting members from the supporting documents; record, reconcile and remit the same as soon as possible. b. Management should give priority to this deficiency with the end in view of eliminating the same from the account balances to ensure reliable financial reports.
observations.managementAction: AAR The Municipal Accountant identified some members and started reconciliation and remittance actions; documents were sent to GSIS for reconciliation.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant already sent requests for reconciliation to the GSIS but GSIS had not yet acted on the requests; remittance and reconciliation pending GSIS response.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management should: a. Require the municipal treasurer to immediately remit all taxes withheld for the accounts of the province and its barangays from various sources within the reglementary period on the 5th day after the end of the quarter. b. Likewise, remit the CRMP shares after the end of each month pursuant to Article F of the Amended Municipal Ordinance No. 10-02 dated August 16, 2010. c. Require the OIC- Municipal Accountant to look into the source documents of the ₱154,353.66 and prepare adjusting entries, if necessary.
observations.managementAction: AAR The LGU remitted the barangay CRM shares. The municipal treasurer remitted the amounts to the barangays and prepared adjusting entries. The Municipal Accountant prepared adjusting entries where necessary.
observations.reason: Documents pertaining to the ₱154,353.66 were yet to be verified; some remittances were made annually instead of by the 5th day after the end of the quarter.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the immediate refund of the total amount of ₱185,764.50. Consequently, those payments spent for the various Fishermen’s Associations may be recovered from them.
observations.managementAction: AAR The municipal accountant advised that the fishermen’s association started paying the ₱185,764.50 through deduction from their monthly CRM share.
observations.reason: The fishermen’s association started paying through deduction from the CRM share; installment payments were ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended for the immediate recovery of the total amount of ₱64,107.40 from the various Fishermen Associations as indicated in the Annex 8. We further recommended that the LGU shall outline programs and projects with long-term benefits which have a direct impact for the sustainable development of its coastal resources.
observations.managementAction: AAR The accountant prepared entries in the subsidiary ledgers for the purpose and recovery has started; the associations started paying on installment basis.
observations.reason: Refund was made on installment basis and some amounts were still outstanding; subsidiary ledgers were prepared.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the following: a. Require concerned officials to immediately refund the disallowed payment amounting to ₱68,000.00. b. Submit the lacking documentary requirements to prevent the suspension of ₱193,417.00 from turning into a disallowance; and c. Desist from attending training seminars organized and/or with the aid of private organizations otherwise this AOM shall serve as a stringent caution that we shall disallow the entire amount should there be any similar occurrences in the future. Take cognizance of DILG MC 2011-59 dated April 25, 2011 strongly discouraging conventions and the like from being held outside of the geographical island where the LGU is located.
observations.managementAction: AAR The Mayor had made partial refunds. Required documents were submitted for verification.
observations.reason: Some barangay officials already made partial refunds; other required documents were submitted for verification.

observations.recommendation: We recommended for the revised and approved SARO specifying in detail the recipients of the PDAF so disbursed and the corresponding Memorandum of Agreement, if so warranted, for proper monitoring and evaluation of the fund transfers, otherwise, we shall be constrained to disallow said payments. Pending compliance, the subject transactions totaling ₱3,400,000.00 are hereby suspended in audit.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor wrote to Congressman Henry Teves sometime in former date and sent a follow up letter dated October 26, 2015 requesting for the revised SARO.
observations.reason: The LGU had not received the revised SARO; follow-up letters were sent to the Congressman but the matter remained pending.

observations.recommendation: We recommended immediate refund in full of the amounts received in excess of what is authorized in DBM Budget Circular No. 2010-3 totalling ₱643,200.00. Direct also the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Budget Officer to be vigilant and not to certify disbursements which go beyond the provisions of the law.
observations.managementAction: AAR Concerned employees already made partial refunds; deducted amounts from payroll. Two employees refunded partially through payroll deduction. One employee (Awayan) had partial refund via payroll deduction and remaining balance to be recovered from terminal pay of Mr. John Rey Rafal while the balance of ₱8,000.00 is a collectible from Ms. Merlita Awayan (deceased).
observations.reason: Two employees had not yet fully refunded the excess amounts; one employee deceased and collection from estate pending; one employee promised to refund from terminal pay.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer intensify collection of real property taxes due from the different delinquent taxpayers by issuing notices of demand for payment of the delinquencies, and to apply the remedies provided under Sections 254 and 258 of the Local Government Code especially against those with delinquent accounts of more than two (2) years if they fail to heed the demand.
observations.managementAction: AAR The Municipal Treasurer issued demand letters to the top 29 delinquent taxpayers and reconciled collections with the iTax system; six taxpayers already entered into compromise agreements.
observations.reason: Some taxpayers entered into compromise agreements; however, large delinquencies remained due to lack of full enforcement of remedies and duplication issues in iTax system.

observations.recommendation: We recommended immediate refund in full of the extra cash gift amounting to ₱1,332,000.00 for CY 2007 and that henceforth, the local chief executive should ensure that the grant of similar benefits complies with prescribed guidelines.
observations.managementAction: AAR Concerned employees made partial refunds and adjustments were made through salary deduction.
observations.reason: Some employees already refunded in part; full collection pending and matter under appeal with COA Regional Office No. VII.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Treasurer prepare properly with diligence the Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms and to submit the same after the end of each month as provided under Sections 50 and 51 of NGAS, Volume II for easy reconciliation and verification of accountable forms and accountability.
observations.managementAction: AAR The Treasurer prepared and submitted the CRAAF and related reports; improvements in reporting procedures instituted.
observations.reason: The Municipal Treasurer-Designate failed to submit the CRAAF within the reglementary period as required in the NGAS Manual due to lack of personnel.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Treasurer prepare and submit to the Municipal Accountant a complete List of Real Property Taxpayers in order that Real Property Tax Receivable and Special Education Tax Receivables will be accurately computed so that amount can be relied upon as the actual amount due for collection.
observations.managementAction: AAR The Municipal Treasurer submitted lists of taxpayers to the Municipal Accountant; the iTax system generated lists but revisions made by the Assessor caused adjustments; reconciliation ongoing.
observations.reason: The list was prepared but general revision in CY 2018 changed the assessment level; reconciliation was ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Accountant strictly comply with the reglementary period prescribed under Section 450 of the GAAM in the matter of the submission of monthly, quarterly and year-end trial balances.
observations.managementAction: AAR The Municipal Accountant took actions to improve timeliness and reporting processes; additional personnel requested.
observations.reason: The accountant did not submit the year-end report on the deadline due to lack of personnel.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the municipality insure the General Insurance Fund of the Government Service Insurance System (GSIS) in order that the government may be indemnified from any hazards incidental to acquisition, transportation and delivery of goods to the place of installation, and against risks of fire, earthquake flood, storm and other calamities.
observations.managementAction: Still awaiting the assessment from the GSIS. The municipality sent out a letter to the GSIS asking for assessment of the project to be insured.
observations.reason: assessment from the GSIS.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LGU procure common supplies from PS-DBM and, henceforth, comply with the provisions of Administrative Order No. 17 dated July 28, 2011 and DBM Circular Letter No. 2011-6 dated August 25, 2011.
observations.managementAction: Available stocks from PS-DBM are limited. In addition, supplies purchased have to be picked up by the procuring entity since PS-DBM has no delivery services, hence, entailing additional costs to the procuring entity.
observations.reason: Available stocks from PS-DBM are limited. In addition, supplies purchased have to be picked up by the procuring entity since PS-DBM has no delivery services, hence, entailing additional costs to the procuring entity.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Solid Waste Material Plan for CY 2015-2024 be immediately prepared and submitted to the National Solid Waste Commission for approval as mandated under Section 16 of RA 9003 for an efficient and effective solid waste collection and disposal.
observations.managementAction: The Local Solid Waste Material Board Chief issued a memorandum. A written request was sent to the concerned department the Provincial heads to prepare the technical assistance from the ENRD in the updating of the ESWM Plan. The SWMB will make a follow-up request for technical assistance in the conduct of WACS in preparation for the updating of the 10-year ESWM.
observations.reason: The SWMB has not prepared the Solid Waste Material Plan. The Local Executive issued a memorandum addressed to the concerned provincial heads. A written request was sent to the department for the regional office for technical assistance.

observations.recommendation: Require the municipal officials and employees to immediately refund in full the extra cash gift amounting to ₱1,332,000.00 for CY 2007. Henceforth, the local chief executive should ensure that the grant of similar benefits complies with prescribed guidelines especially insofar as observance of the PS limitation is concerned so that the disbursements will not be disallowed in audit.
observations.managementAction: The amount has been fully refunded by former treasurer Mr. Romer Delfino in CY 2017. An appeal with COA Regional Office No. VII is ongoing.
observations.reason: The disallowance is under appeal with COA Regional Office No. VII.

observations.recommendation: Require the treasurer to prepare and submit to the municipal accountant a complete List of Real Property Taxpayers in order that Real Property Tax and Special Education Tax Receivables will be accurately computed so that the amount can be relied upon as the actual amount due for tax collection.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer prepared and submitted the List of Taxpayers. Verification by the MTO is still on-going.
observations.reason: Verification by MTO is still on-going.

observations.recommendation:
RECOMMENDATION:
We recommended that management undertake a concerted effort to
implement PPAs outlined under the 20% DF and hasten the completion of the
projects so that the constituents can enjoy the benefits that can be derived from the
projects.

RECOMMENDATIONS:
We recommended that in the preparation of its Annual Work and Financial
Plan, the LCPC allocate more funds for PPAs that would promote children’s right
and welfare and protect them against abuse, neglect, exploitation and violence as
envisioned under R.A. 9344. We further recommended that the Council prepare
the Local Development and Investment Plans for Children, Local Code for
Children, and Local State of Children’s Report as required under DILG
Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120 dated July 4, 2012.

RECOMMENDATIONS:
We recommended and management agreed that the MAO (i) closely
supervise the disposal program, seeing to it that complete records are kept
including all details of births offspring, deaths and transfers; (ii) submit to the
Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountantna monthly report of births of
animals; (iii) facilitate the request for relief of accountability in case of death or
disposal, or require payment of the value of the animal; and (iv) exert more
effort in determining the current recipient and the status of the 22 heads of
cattle.

We further recommended that the Municipal Treasurer post the monthly
report of births of animals to the property records and the Municial Accountant
to the books of accounts.

We also recommended that the MAO, Municipal Treasurer and Municipal
Accountant reconcile their records taking into consideration the physical
inventory as of December 31, 2017, and make necessary adjustments.

RECOMMENDATION:
We recommended that management ensure that only development projects
are charged to the fund in accordance with DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum
Circular No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017.

RECOMMENDATION:
We recommended and management agreed to comply with the limitations
and regulations on the grant of Uniform/Clothing Allowance, whether in cash or in
kind, currently set at ₱5,000.00 per annum pursuant to the pertinent provisions of
the GAA and Budget Circular No. 2012-1 dated February 23, 2012.

RECOMMENDATIONS:
We recommended that management:
a. Prioritize the allocation of funds for the establishment and operation of a
sanitary landfill in accordance with Sections 40 and 41 of R.A. 9003. In the
meantime, take action to convert the existing open dump site into a controlled
dump and hasten its closure within a specific time frame;
b. Enforce strictly the Municipality’s Ordinances relative to solid waste
management and ensure 100 percent compliance thereof; and
c. Involve all barangays to actively participate in the implementation of waste
reduction and segregation at source in accordance with RA No. 9003.

We recommended that the LGU require the Municipal Engineer to conduct regular inspections of the project for the contractor to undertake immediate repair, in case of damage or defect. We also recommended that LGU set aside funds to construct canal on both sides of the project to avoid further deterioration of the concrete pavements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Engineer conducted follow up inspections of the projects completed starting CY 2017.
observations.reason: through the Municipal Engineer conducted follow up inspections of the projects starting CY 2017.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LGU conduct an industry forum and regular/special registration to gather data on the availability of jobs and jobseekers who are possible target beneficiaries.
observations.managementAction: The Local Economic and Investment Promotion Officer (LEIPO)-Designate conducted a follow up the approval of the Workforce Development Plan (WFD) from DILG, Regional Office No. VII, Cebu City.
observations.reason: Conduct of trainings were not completely implemented as scheduled.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management immediately dispose of the ten (10) units of unserviceable motor vehicles in accordance with the procedures set forth for the disposal under Section 79 of PD No. 1445 to avoid further deterioration and possible loss of value.
observations.managementAction: The DPWH has not sent any personnel yet who will conduct appraisal.
observations.reason: The DPWH has not sent any personnel yet to conduct the appraisal of the ten (10) unserviceable motor vehicles.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant review the accounts payables which have been outstanding for more than two (2) years, and if found to have no valid claims against the municipality, cause the reversion of the same to the government equity.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal payable amounting to ₱89,539.00 was already reverted in CY 2016. The Municipal Accountant waited for a valid claimant to these amounts.
observations.reason: Still awaiting claimant to these amounts. Since there are no available documents to support the reconciling items the amounts remained unadjusted as of December 31, 2017.

observations.recommendation: We recommended further that they make representation and seek assistance from LTO, Dumaguete City, for the immediate processing of the registration of the vehicle.
observations.managementAction: The DPWH has inquired and has not yet sent any personnel to conduct the registration. The Municipality has inquired to LTO Regional Office No. VII, Cebu City for the requirements needed for the registration of the ambulance. The municipality waited for the registration of the donated ambulance.
observations.reason: Lack of personnel who could focus on the processing of the registration of the ambulance.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Sangguniang Bayan pass a resolution authorizing and regulating the purchase and assignment of mobile phones, specifically identifying the officials who shall be entitled to postpaid plan, and setting forth ceilings and limitations as to the amount of monthly allowance for each official. We further recommended that those with more than one (1) unit including those acquired from previous plans shall return the same to the Office of the Municipal Treasurer.
observations.managementAction: The Sangguniang Bayan has already passed a resolution in CY 2017.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the BAC post in the PhilGEPS agency website, if any the Notice of Award, Notice of Proceed and Approved Contract as well as notify the losing bidders the result of the bidding pursuant to Sections 37.1.2, 37.1.6 and 37.4.2 of the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA 9184.
