Conduct survey, site, construction materials, soils and foundation investigations on the road concreting projects, to ascertain the soundness and adequacy of materials/goods to be procured and invite only associations from a duly recognized group in a sector or discipline relevant to the procurement at hand.
observations.managementAction: Sometimes, the municipality posted the Notice of Award at the PhilGEPS website. The posting is not conspicuous due to the very slow internet connection.
observations.reason: posting in the PhilGEPS is not conspicuous completed due to the municipality’s internet connection.

observations.recommendation: a. The Inventory Committee be required to conduct the physical count of properties and reconcile the same with related property and accounting records and the municipal treasurer to maintain the property cards in accordance with existing regulations and to locate the missing movable assets listed under Annex 1 of this report. b. The Accountant to reclassify the fully depreciated and unserviceable assets from the Property Plant and Equipment to Other Asset accounts and to locate the disbursement vouchers of the unbooked motor vehicles, IT Equipment and furniture and fixtures so these can be recorded in the books.
observations.managementAction: The audit team received a copy of the RPCPPE last April 25, 2017 without the complete AAR information of 2013 to 2016. The report was not signed by the Inventory Committee nor approved by the LCE.
observations.reason: The report was not signed by the Inventory Committee nor approved by the LCE.

observations.recommendation: The Municipal Accountant and the Treasurer be required to return the unexpended balances of cash received for completed projects to the source national government agencies and/or remit the unexpended PDAF/CDF to the National Treasury pursuant to Section 6.7 of COA Circular 94-013 and the Supreme Court decision dated November 19, 2013 under General Ruling Numbers 208566, 208493 and 209251.
observations.managementAction: Balances of cash received from national government agencies totaling ₱396,552.41 was already sent to the national treasury. The Municipal Accountant already sent out the ORs to the national treasury. The amounts were remitted to the national treasury. The Municipal Accountant already sent out the documents to the national treasury.
observations.reason: Partially Implemented

observations.recommendation: a. The Municipal Accountant be required to reclassify the recorded Advances to Officers and Employees totalling ₱61,876.68 to Other Receivables. b. Proper coordination with the Department of Education (DepEd) be made to require the DepEd officials who are also members of the Local School Board to secure clearances from money and property accountabilities from the concerned local government agency, where they are assigned, to ensure that all outstanding cash advances and other property accountabilities are settled and/or returned first before they retire or transfer.
observations.managementAction: Due to lack of coordination between LGU and DepEd personnel, outstanding cash advances were not fully settled. The proposed reclassification entries are as follows: Other Receivables P61,876.68 Due from Officers & Employees P61,876.68. The Municipal Accountant already sent demand letters; however, a few accountable persons have not yet settled their accounts.
observations.reason: Due to lack of coordination between LGU and DepEd personnel, outstanding cash advances were not fully settled.

observations.recommendation: a. The Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to maintain their respective property records for all real property. b. The Mayor create a committee to process the transfer of title of all acquired real properties to the municipal government. c. The Mayor request the Sangguniang Bayan for appropriations to cover the cost of titling of all real properties.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor issued a memorandum creating a committee to process the titling of land owned by the LGU.
observations.reason: The committee is still processing the titling of land owned by the LGU.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: a. The Accountant and Treasurer exert all efforts to locate the missing disbursement vouchers amounting to ₱117,360.76 and the unidentified reconciling items of ₱1,973,457.26 in order that these can be taken up in the books by adjusting journal entries. b. For the other reconciling items, the Municipal Accountant take up the following adjusting journal entries: For the erroneous deposit: Debit Prior Year’s Adjustment 5,826.38 Credit Due to Officers & Employees 5,826.38 For the bank charges: Debit Prior Year‘s Adjustments 350.00 Credit Cash in Bank 350.00 For the Stale check: Debit Cash in Bank 33,313.85 Credit Accounts Payable/Other Appropriate Account 33,313.85
observations.managementAction: The amount of reconciling items ₱117,360.76 was paid by former Municipal Treasurer Mr. Romer Delfino. These items were already taken up in the books in CY 2017.
observations.reason: The unidentified reconciling items amounting to ₱1,973,457.26 remained unadjusted as of December 31, 2017. The reconciling items existed before CY 2000 and there are no available documents to support the reconciliation.

observations.recommendation: a. Adopt measures to mitigate the effects of the current open dumpsite. b. Implement the Solid Waste Management program as soon as possible in order to protect the health of the constituents as well as preserve the environment. c. Request the members of the Sangguniang Bayan for the inclusion in the Supplemental Budget or CY 2015 Annual Budget enough appropriations for the Solid Waste Management Programs and its activities.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor has been addressing the issue. The municipality is still operating an open dumpsite because of limited resources. The municipality is considering buying equipment to convert wastes into useful materials.
observations.reason: Because of limited resources the municipality is still operating an open dumpsite.

observations.recommendation: a. Require the Bids and Awards Committee to identify and sort out in the PPMP and APP the common-use supplies and procure the same at PS-DBM. b. See to it that the procurement of goods and services not available at PS-DBM are done through public bidding except where allowed under R.A. 9184.
observations.managementAction: The municipality procured common goods, supplies and materials from private suppliers due to lack of available stocks at PS-DBM. Hence, it resorted to bidding.
observations.reason: The LGU procured from private suppliers due to lack of available stocks at PS-DBM.

observations.recommendation: a. Send immediately demand letters to the concerned accountable officials/employees. b. If the employees concerned still fail to settle their cash advances after due notice, instruct the municipal treasurer to withhold the payment of their salaries. c. Require in particular the Municipal Accountant and other concerned officials/employees to strictly observe the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 in the grant, utilization and liquidation of cash advances.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant already sent demand letters; however, a few accountable persons have not yet settled their accounts. It is also noteworthy that national agency personnel are not required to get a clearance from the LGU thus making it difficult to compel settlement of the accounts.
observations.reason: Some outstanding cash advances were not fully settled; national agency personnel are not required to get clearance from the LGU making it difficult to compel settlement.

observations.recommendation: a. The Inventory Committee should conduct the physical count of breeding stocks under the custody of the Municipal Agriculture Office. b. The Inventory Committee should furnish the Municipal Accountant the result of the physical count of breeding stocks. c. Require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to keep and regularly maintain/update the required ledger and stock cards. d. Require the offices concerned to reconcile their respective records and make necessary adjustment of the accounts, if necessary.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer were required to maintain a WOABSLC and stock card but both records are still not reconciled.
observations.reason: Both records are still not reconciled.

observations.recommendation: a. The accountant should locate and trace/identify remitting members from the supporting documents; reconcile and remit the same as soon as possible. b. Management should give priority to this deficiency with the end in view of eliminating the same from the account balances to ensure reliable financial reports.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant has neither identified under whose account the unremitted amounts belong nor the remitting members. To date, the GSIS has neither acted on the request of the LGU.
observations.reason: The LGU has not identified the remitting members under whose account the unremitted amounts belong. The GSIS has neither acted on the request of the LGU.

observations.recommendation: a. Require the municipal treasurer to immediately remit all taxes withheld to the province and its barangays from various sources within the reglementary period on the 5th day after the end of the quarter. b. Likewise, remit the CRMP shares after the end of each month pursuant to Article F of the Amended Municipal Ordinance No. 10-02 dated August 16, 2010. c. Require the OIC-Municipal Accountant to look into the source documents of the ₱154,353.66 and prepare adjusting entries, if necessary.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal remitted the barangay shares. The RPT and CRM remittances to the barangays are done annually instead of the 5th day after the end of the quarter. Documents pertaining to the ₱154,353.66 are yet to be verified.
observations.reason: RPT and CRM remittances to barangays are done annually instead of the 5th day after the end of the quarter. Documents pertaining to ₱154,353.66 are yet to be verified.

observations.recommendation: We recommended for the immediate refund of the total amount of ₱185,764.50. Consequently, those spent for the various Fishermen Associations may be recovered from them.
observations.managementAction: The association has started paying the amount as indicated in the annex. The association refunded the amount through their CRM share deduction every month.
observations.reason: The association has started the payment of the amount because the payment is on installment deducted from their CRM share every month.

observations.recommendation: We recommended for the immediate recovery of the total amount of ₱64,107.40 from the various Fishermen Associations as indicated in Annex 8. We further recommended that the LGU shall endeavor to outline programs and projects with long-term benefits which have a direct impact for the sustainable development of its coastal resources.
observations.managementAction: The association has started the payment and has recorded the amounts as indicated in the annex. The association is paying on installment deducted from their CRM share every month.
observations.reason: The association has started paying the amount because the payment is on installment deducted from their CRM share every month.

observations.recommendation: The following: a. Require concerned officials to refund immediately in full the disallowed payment amounting to ₱68,000.00. b. Submit the lacking documentary requirements to prevent the suspension of ₱193,417.00 from turning into a disallowance; and, c. Desist from attending training seminars organized and/or with the aid of private organizations, otherwise, this AOM shall serve as a stringent caution that we shall disallow the entire amount should there be any similar occurrences in the future. Take cognizance of DILG MC 2011-59 dated April 25, 2011 strongly discouraging conventions and the like from being held outside of the geographical island where the LGU is located.
observations.managementAction: The barangay officials made partial refunds. The municipal mayor has already partially refunded the disallowed amount.
observations.reason: The barangay officials made partial refunds; some attendance in seminars discouraged in Negros Oriental.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management provide the auditor the revised and approved SARO specifying in detail the corresponding recipients of the PDAF disbursements and the Memorandum of Agreement, if so warranted, for proper monitoring and evaluation of the fund transfers, otherwise, we shall be constrained to disallow said payments. Pending compliance, the subject transactions totaling ₱3,400,000.00 are hereby suspended in audit.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal has not received the revised SARO. The Municipal Mayor sometime in CY 2014 followed up the revised SARO. Another letter dated October 26, 2015 was sent to Congressman Teves as a follow-up.
observations.reason: The municipality has not received the revised SARO to date.

observations.recommendation: a. Immediate refund in full of the amounts received in excess of what is authorized in DBM Budget Circular No. 2010-3 totalling ₱643,200.00. b. Direct also the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Budget Officer to be vigilant and not to certify disbursements which go beyond the provisions of the law.
observations.managementAction: There are two (2) employees who have not yet fully refunded the amount. The municipality has been making partial refunds through payroll deduction.
observations.reason: There are two (2) employees who have not yet fully refunded the amount because the payment is on installment deduction from payroll.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer intensify collection of real property taxes due from the different delinquent taxpayers by collecting real property taxes. Issue notices of demand for payment of the delinquencies, and to apply the remedies provided under Sections 254 and 258 of the Local Government Code especially against those with delinquent accounts of more than two (2) years if they fail to heed the demand.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer has intensified collection. The amount has been increased to ₱6,414,220.87 as of December 31, 2017. Apart from intensifying collection, a contributing factor in the increase in the Real Property Tax Receivable is the water system where the applicant cannot apply for water connection without paying the RPT.
observations.reason: Up to this time, Real Property Tax Receivable reached ₱6,414,220.87. Aside from the effort to intensify collection, another contributing factor is the water system requirement for RPT payment before water connection.

observations.recommendation: To enable the auditor to inspect, review and evaluate the transactions/projects on time, we recommended that management require the municipal officials concerned to submit copies of the delivery documents within twenty-four (24) hours from acceptance of goods and services thereof.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal already submitted the 2007 delivery documents to COA starting CY 2018.
observations.reason: Copies of the 2007 delivery documents were submitted for inspection and review starting CY 2018.

observations.recommendation: Require the concerned treasurer to produce the duly approved disbursements and supporting documents to justify the check issuances; otherwise, require him to refund the amount as disbursed.
observations.managementAction: The amount has been fully refunded by former treasurer Mr. Romer Delfino in CY 2017.

observations.recommendation: Require the treasurer and collectors to prepare the Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms and the CRAAF and submit the same after the end of each month as provided under Sections 50 and 51 of the NGAS, Volume II for easy reconciliation and verification of accountable forms and accountability thereof.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer-Designate failed to submit monthly the year end report on the statement of accountability. The treasurer already submitted some reports.
observations.reason: The Municipal Treasurer-Designate failed to submit the year end report on the statement of accountability within the required period.

observations.recommendation: Direct the Municipal Accountant to strictly comply with the reglementary period prescribed under Section 450 of the GAAM in the submission of monthly, quarterly and year-end trial balances.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant has not submitted the year end report on the trial balance. The Municipal Accountant submitted some trial balances but not on time.
observations.reason: The Accountant has not submitted the year end report on the trial balance as of the reporting date.

observations.recommendation: Require the municipal officials and employees to immediately refund in full the extra cash gift amounting to P1,332,000.00 for CY 2007. Henceforth, the local chief executive should ensure that the grant of similar benefits complies with prescribed guidelines especially insofar as observance of the PS limitation is concerned so that the disbursements will not be disallowed in audit.
observations.reason: The disallowance is under appeal with COA Regional Office No. VII.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Agricultural Officer to require the farmers association to use the solar dryers, otherwise, find ways and means to utilize solar dryers for productive activities that would redound to the benefit of the constituents of Barangay Boloc-boloc and Malungcay Dacu.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Agriculture Officer met with the local farmers association who agreed to use the two (2) solar dryers which are now operational after minor repair work was done.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the municipality immediately stop the hiring of a private lawyer. Instead, the municipality should secure the services of the Provincial Legal Officer or the Provincial Fiscal's Office to handle their legal cases.
observations.managementAction: The municipality immediately stopped the services of a private lawyer. Also, the amount of ₱36,000.00 was returned to the coffers of the municipality.

observations.reason: Posting in the PhilGEPS the Notice of Award at the website is still in process due to the slow internet connection.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the municipality immediately prepare the Solid Waste Material Plan for CY 2015-2024 and submit it to the National Solid Waste Commission to prepare the Solid Waste Material Plan as mandated under Section 16 of RA 9003 for an efficient and effective solid waste collection and disposal.
observations.managementAction: The Local Chief Executive issued a memorandum and the municipal treasurer and the municipal accountant to maintain their respective property records. The SWMB was instructed to prepare the Solid Waste Material Plan but has not prepared the Solid Waste Material Plan to date.
observations.reason: The SWMB has not prepared the Solid Waste Material Plan to date.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive always represent the municipality in all its contracts and sign on its behalf all contracts, obligations and other documents pursuant to Section 444(b)(vi) of R.A. 7160.
observations.managementAction: The Local Chief Executive implemented that all contracts shall pass to his office duly noted and approved by him.

The Inventory Committee be required to conduct the physical count of properties and reconcile the same with related property and accounting records and to maintain property cards in accordance with existing regulations and b. The Accountant to reclassify the fully depreciated and unserviceable assets from the Property Plant and Equipment to Other Asset accounts and to locate the disbursement vouchers of the unbooked motor vehicles, IT Equipment and furniture and fixtures so these can be recorded in the books.

observations.reason: The report was not signed by the Committee nor approved by the LCE.

observations.recommendation: a. The BAC and BAC Secretariat strictly comply with the provisions of RA 9184 and the Procurement Law in order to obtain the most advantageous price for the municipality. b. The persons responsible for this transaction be required to provide explanation/justification for the deficiencies noted. c. The Municipal Accounting Office be advised to ensure that all claims are duly supported with all the necessary documents before payment to prevent audit suspension or disallowance.
observations.managementAction: The LGU attached the documents stated in the AOM complied with the provisions of RA 9184, specifically the posting of Notice of Awards, Notice to Proceed and approved contract in the PhilGEPS.

observations.recommendation: The Municipal Accountant and the Treasurer be required to return the unexpended balances of cash received for completed projects to the source agency/National Treasury and/or remit the unexpended PDAF/CDF to the National Treasury pursuant to Section 6.7 of COA Circular 94-013 and the Supreme Court decision dated November 19, 2013 under General Ruling Numbers 208566, 208493 and 209251.
observations.managementAction: Balances of cash received from the Bureau of Treasury on 4/15/15 per OR ₱396,552.41 were deposited to the Bureau of Treasury but were not remitted to the source agency because the National Treasury requires the identification of sources of funds.
observations.reason: The national treasury requires the identification of sources of funds.

observations.recommendation: a. The Municipal Accountant be required to reclassify the recorded Advances to Officers and Employees totalling P61,876.68 to Other Receivables. b. Proper coordination with the Department of Education (DepEd) be made to require the DepEd officials who are also members of the Local School Board to secure clearances from money and property accountabilities from the concerned local government agency, where they are assigned, to ensure that all outstanding cash advances and other property accountabilities are settled and/or returned first before they retire or transfer.
observations.managementAction: The proposed reclassification entries are as follows: Other Receivables P61,876.68 / Due from Officers & Employees P61,876.68  Coordination with DepEd was undertaken but outstanding cash advances were not fully settled.
observations.reason: Due to lack of coordination between LGU and DepEd personnel, outstanding cash advances were not fully settled.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant should ensure that payments for repairs and maintenance of motor vehicles be supported with the documents as required under Paragraph 9.1.3.4 of the COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.
observations.managementAction: The municipality attached the supporting documents to DVs.

observations.recommendation: a. The Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to maintain their respective property records for all real property. b. The Mayor create a committee to process the transfer of title of all acquired real properties to the municipal government. c. The Mayor request the Sangguniang Bayan for appropriations to cover the cost of titling of all real properties.
observations.managementAction: The LCE issued a memorandum creating a committee to process the titling. The committee is still processing the titling of land owned by the LGU.
observations.reason: The Committee is still processing the titling.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: a. The Accountant and Treasurer exert all efforts to locate the disbursement vouchers amounting to ₱117,360.76 and the unidentified disbursement items of ₱1,973,457.26 in order that these can be taken up in the books accordingly. b. For the other reconciling items, the Municipal Accountant take up the necessary adjusting journal entries as provided.
observations.managementAction: The LCE issued a memorandum exhorting the Municipal Treasurer and Accountant to exert efforts to reconcile the difference.
observations.reason: The unidentified reconciling items remained unadjusted as of December 31, 2016.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the municipality: a. Adopt measures to mitigate the effects of the current open dumpsite. b. Implement the Solid Waste Management program as soon as possible in order to protect the health of the constituents as well as preserve the environment. c. Request the members of the Sangguniang Bayan for the inclusion in the Supplemental Budget or CY 2015 Annual Budget enough appropriations for the Solid Waste Management Programs and its activities.
observations.managementAction: The municipality instituted measures by initiating the enclosure of the open dumpsite.
observations.reason: San Jose, Sibulan, Dumaguete City, Bacong, Valencia, Dauin and Zamboanguita are planning to form a cluster for the establishment of a sanitary landfill in Valencia.

observations.recommendation: a. Require the Bids and Awards Committee to identify and sort out in the PPMP the common-use supplies and procure the same from PS-DBM pursuant to Administrative Order No. 17. b. See to it that the procurement of goods and services not available at PS-DBM are done through public bidding except where allowed under R.A. 9184.
observations.managementAction: The municipality procured common goods, supplies and materials from private suppliers due to lack of available stocks at PS-DBM and hence resorted to bidding.
observations.reason: DBM Bacolod depot is upgrading its system and PS-DBM stocks were not available; hence, resort to private suppliers.

observations.recommendation: a. The Municipal Treasurer intensify real property tax collection efforts to provide additional sources of revenue. b. The Local Finance Committee study and explore possibilities of increasing local revenue sources and fees to minimize the municipality's dependence on IRA.
observations.managementAction: The LGU enacted a new tax ordinance implemented in CY 2016 which resulted in an increase in revenue for the period.

observations.recommendation: a. Send immediately demand letters to the all concerned accountable agency/other agency officials/employees. b. If the employees concerned still fail to settle their cash advances after due notice, instruct the municipal treasurer to withhold the payment of their salaries. c. Require in particular the Municipal Accountant and other concerned officials/employees to strictly observe the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 in the grant, utilization and liquidation of cash advances.
observations.managementAction: The municipal employees have already been sent demand letters to all concerned.
observations.reason: The employees have not yet fully settled their cash advances.

observations.recommendation: a. The Inventory Committee should conduct the physical count of breeding stocks under the WOABSLC and furnish the Municipal Accountant the result of the physical count. b. Require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to keep and regularly maintain/update the required ledger and stock cards. c. Require the offices concerned to reconcile their respective records and make necessary adjustment of the accounts, if necessary.
observations.managementAction: The Inventory Committee was advised to conduct the physical count of breeding stocks and the Municipal Agriculture Office to assist.
observations.reason: Both records are still not reconciled.

observations.recommendation: a. The accountant should locate and trace/identify remitting members from the supporting documents; reconcile and remit the same as soon as possible. b. Management should give priority to this deficiency with the end in view of eliminating the same from the account balances to ensure reliable financial reports.
observations.managementAction: The municipal accountant has not yet identified under whose account the unremitted amount belong.
observations.reason: To date, the GSIS has neither acted on the request of the LGU.

observations.recommendation: a. Require the municipal treasurer to immediately remit all withheld shares of the province and its barangays from various sources within the reglementary period on the 5th day after the end of the quarter. b. Likewise, remit the CRMP shares after the end of each month pursuant to Article F of the Amended Municipal Ordinance No. 10-02 dated August 16, 2010. c. Require the OIC-Municipal Accountant to look into the source documents of the P154,353.66 and prepare adjusting entries, if necessary.
observations.managementAction: The LGU remitted the barangay remittances annually instead of remitting on the 5th day after the end of the quarter. Documents pertaining to the ₱154,353.66 are yet to be verified.
observations.reason: Documents pertaining to the ₱154,353.66 are yet to be verified.

observations.recommendation: It was recommended the immediate refund of the total amount of P185,764.50. Consequently, those spent for the various Fishermen Associations may be recovered from them.
observations.managementAction: The fishermen's association started the payment of the amount of ₱185,764.50 refunded the amount due in installment thru deduction from the CRM share every month.
observations.reason: The association started to pay the amount refunded in installment; not fully paid.

observations.recommendation: We recommended for the immediate recovery of the total amount of P64,107.40 from the various Fishermen Associations as indicated in Annex 8. We further recommended that the LGU shall endeavor to outline programs and projects with long-term benefits which have a direct impact for the sustainable development of its coastal resources.
observations.managementAction: The association has started the payment of the amount due and duly recorded by the accountant in the subsidiary ledgers prepared for the purpose.
observations.reason: The association has not fully paid the amount.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: a. The LGU should submit the documents stipulated in Annex 10 for further evaluation of the payments made. b. The Municipal Accountant, as the internal auditor of the LGU, be reminded to meticulously look into the completeness of the required and proper documents supporting claims against the government, pursuant to Sec. 4(6) of P.D. No. 1445 in order to substantiate the validity and propriety of the payments, and to avoid possible audit suspensions and disallowances.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant submitted the required documents. Succeeding vouchers were properly supported with documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the following: a. Require concerned officials to refund immediately in full the disallowed payment amounting to P68,000.00. b. Submit the lacking documentary requirements to prevent the suspension of P193,417.00 from turning into a disallowance; and, c. Desist from attending training seminars organized and/or with the aid of private organizations, otherwise, this AOM shall serve as a stringent caution that we shall disallow the entire amount should there be any similar occurrences in the future. Take cognizance of DILG MC 2011-59 dated April 25, 2011 strongly discouraging conventions and the like from being held outside of the geographical island where the LGU is located.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor has discouraged attendance in seminars organized by non-accredited private institutions. The barangay officials made partial refunds.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the municipality provide the auditor the revised and approved SARO specifying in detail the recipients of the PDAF disbursed and the corresponding Memorandum of Agreement, if so warranted, for proper monitoring and evaluation of the fund transfers; otherwise, we shall be constrained to disallow said payments.
observations.managementAction: The Local Chief Executive/Municipal Mayor has written the former Congressman Henry Teves sometime last year (2014) and a follow-up letter dated Oct. 26, 2015 was sent. Another letter dated April 15, 2016 was sent to follow-up the revised SARO. To date, the revised SARO has not been received.
observations.reason: Pending compliance, the subject transactions totaling P3,400,000.00 are hereby suspended in audit.

observations.recommendation: a. We recommended the immediate refund in full of the amounts received in excess of what is authorized in DBM Budget Circular No. 2010-3. b. Direct also the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Budget Officer to be vigilant and not to certify disbursements which go beyond the provisions of the law.
observations.managementAction: Employees have started making partial refunds thru payroll deduction.
observations.reason: Employees have not yet fully refunded the amount.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer intensify collection of real property taxes due from the delinquent property taxpayers by issuing notices of demand for payment of delinquencies, and to apply the remedies provided under Sections 254 and 258 of the Local Government Code especially against those with delinquent accounts of more than two (2) years if they fail to heed the demand.
observations.managementAction: The municipal accountant and collectors intensified collection efforts of real property taxes and issued notices/demand for payment as required. Real Property Tax Receivable reached ₱5,928,494.47 as of December 31, 2016.

observations.recommendation: To enable the auditor to inspect, review and evaluate the transactions/projects on time, we recommended that management require municipal officials concerned to submit copies of the delivery documents within twenty-four (24) hours from acceptance of goods and services.
observations.managementAction: Delivery documents are submitted only for major procurements.
observations.reason: Delivery documents are only submitted for major procurements.

observations.recommendation: Require the concerned treasurer to produce the duly approved disbursements and their supporting documents to justify the check issuances; otherwise, require him to refund the amount as disbursed.
observations.managementAction: The matter is still on-going; the former Municipal Treasurer retired effective January 24, 2016.
observations.reason: The former Municipal Treasurer retired effective January 24, 2016.

observations.recommendation: Require the treasurer and collectors to prepare properly and with diligence the Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms and to submit the same after the end of each month as provided under Sections 50 and 51 of the NGAS, Volume II for easy reconciliation and verification of accountable forms and accountability thereof.
observations.reason: The former Municipal Treasurer failed to submit the CRAAF within the reglementary period.

observations.recommendation: Require the municipal treasurer to prepare and submit to the municipal accountant a complete List of Real Property Taxpayers in order that Real Property Tax and Special Education Tax Receivables will be accurately computed so that amount can be relied upon as the actual amount due for collection.

Direct the Municipal Accountant to strictly comply with the reglementary period prescribed under Section 450 of the GAAM in the matter of the submission of monthly, quarterly and year-end trial balances.
observations.managementAction: The Accountant is still familiarizing with the PPSAS and has not yet submitted the year end report on the stipulated deadline.
observations.reason: The Accountant is still familiarizing with the PPSAS.
